Angelhelm.description:
Good-aligned wearer only. Melee attacks are treated as good-aligned weapons for overcoming damage reduction (continuous, no activation).

In addition, once per day each: cure critical wounds, dispel evil (DC 17), and resist energy (acid or cold only) as spell-like abilities.

Grants no benefit to nongood wearers.

Helm of Brilliance.description: Diamonds and spells; detonates if wearer dies.

Helm of Comprehend Languages and Read Ma.description: Comprehend languages and read magic while worn.

Helm of Underwater Action.description: Continuous speak with animals (aquatic only), water breathing, freedom of movement in water.

Bear helm.description:
While raging, you can halve the damage dealt by a single sneak attack or critical hit scored against you. You can activate this after the damage from the attack has been determined.

Functions once per day.

Beholder crown.description: Ten eyelike gems each fire a different ray once (ranged touch, 30 ft.): charm monster, charm person, deep slumber, disintegrate, fear, finger of death, flesh to stone, inflict moderate wounds, slow, and telekinesis (violent thrust, up to 325 lb.; Will DC 17 negates). Using a ray burns out that gem.

Circlet of mages.description: This leather circlet has a silver headpiece showing crescent moons on either side of a seven-pointed star. A circlet of mages grants you a +2 competence bonus on Concentration checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, this circlet has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges when you cast a spell allows you to avoid losing that prepared spell or spell slot (as if you hadn’t cast it). 1 charge: Retain a spell of up to 1st level. 2 charges: Retain a spell of up to 2nd level. 3 charges: Retain a spell of up to 3rd level.

Circlet of rapid casting.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges allows you to cast a single spell as part of the same swift action used to activate the circlet. The spell must normally have a casting time of no longer than 1 standard action.

1 charge: Cast a spell of up to 2nd level.
2 charges: Cast a spell of up to 3rd level.
3 charges: Cast a spell of up to 4th level.

Circlet of solace.description:
After activating the circlet, the next healing spell you cast on a living ally before the end of your turn also grants that creature a +5 morale bonus on saves against disease, fear, and poison for 1 minute. If the spell affects multiple creatures, only one (your choice) gains this benefit.

Functions three times per day.

Circlet of wild surging.description: This thin, platinum circlet depicts blasts of wild energy across its surface. A circlet of wild surging allows you to enhance your emotional state to make your wild surges more powerful. When you use wild surge while manifesting a power, you can activate the circlet to increase the bonus to your manifester level by 1. The increased wild surge still requires the normal extra power point cost normally required to augment the power. This also increases your chance of psychic enervation by 5% and increases the duration of your surging euphoria by 1 round (if you have that class feature). A circlet of wild surging functions three times per day, but won’t function in consecutive rounds.

Crown of flamesSP.description: Deals 2d6 fire damage to attackers who hit the wearer in melee 1/day.

Essentia helm.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges maximizes essentia investment in soulmelds or other essentia receptacles for 1 round. While active, you cannot reallocate essentia from affected receptacles.

1 charge: One receptacle.
2 charges: Two receptacles.
3 charges: Three receptacles.

Hat of anonymity.description: While you wear it, you are under a continuous nondetection effect and have a +5 bonus on Hide checks.

Headband of conscious effort.description: While wearing this headband, you can attempt a Concentration check instead of a required Fortitude saving throw once per day.

Headband of the lorebinder.description: The face of this blue-green metal circlet is stamped with the image of a blank, open scroll. A headband of the lorebinder grants a +4 sacred bonus on bardic knowledge checks (and on a loremaster’s lore checks). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. Three times per day, you can activate the headband to gain the effect of a read magic spell. This ability lasts for 30 minutes.

Helm of battle.description:
When activated, gain a +2 bonus on the next attack roll or ability check you make to attempt a bull rush, disarm, overrun, sunder, or trip during your turn.

Functions three times per day.

Helm of gazes.description: This mirror-bright steel cap is set with three tiger’s-eye gems, each one looking in a different direction. Each time you would be required to roll a save against a gaze attack, a helm of gazes automatically absorbs the gaze (with no action required). This ability functions three times per day.

Helm of glorious recovery.description: This magnifi cent helmet is chased with gold and silver, and set with three yellow topazes. Developed by a militant, lawful good sect for its paladins, these helmets are potent healing devices. Once per day, this helmet can heal you of 4d8+7 points of damage.

Helm of heroes.description:
When you wear this helm and use your grant move action ability, you can choose instead to grant each affected character a single melee attack (using each ally's full base attack bonus). This activation is part of the grant move action activation.

Functions once per day.

Helm of tacticsSP.description: Grants a +2 competence bonus on attack rolls when flanking 1/day.

Helm of the hunterSP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Spot checks 1/day.

Helm of the purple plumeR.description:
While wearing this helm, you have a +2 enhancement bonus to Charisma if you are lawful good, neutral good, or lawful neutral.

Relic power (worship Heironeous and sacrifice a 4th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 7 HD): +5 morale bonus on saves against fear. Whenever you successfully save against a fear effect, you receive the benefit of heroism for 10 rounds. If immune to fear, you automatically gain this benefit when subjected to a fear effect from an enemy.

Helm of wounding sightSP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Spot checks to find wounded creatures 1/day.

Horned helm.description: When placed on your head, the antlers join solidly with your skull. They grant a natural secondary gore attack dealing 1d8 damage plus half your Strength bonus, treated as a magic weapon for overcoming damage reduction.

Phoenix helm.description: This helm is forged of copper and inlaid with silver and gold. The whole piece is stylized in the shape of a phoenix with its wings unfurled, its legs guarding the temples, and its head adorning the brow. A phoenix helm grants you low-light vision. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, you can use each of the following abilities once per day: feather fall, resist energy, and speak with animals (birds only). Each of these abilities can be used as a standard (command) action except feather fall, which requires an immediate (command) action to activate.

Scout’s headband.description: Piercer Rearguard’s Scry cloak cape shroud This tattered strip of cloth is strangely resilient, and when you hold it, elven runes appear along its length. A scout’s headband grants you a +2 competence bonus on Spot checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, this circlet has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges enhances your visual acuity as described below. 1 charge: You gain darkvision out to 60 feet for 1 hour. 2 charges: You can see invisible creatures and objects (as see invisibility) for 10 minutes. 3 charges: You gain true seeing (as the spell) for 1 minute. Lore: The runes appearing on the headband are taken from the Saga of Filix, a great elf scout from long ago (Knowledge [history] DC 10). Filix bargained with Corellon for unparalleled powers of vision, but the gift came with a curse: The scout could see the deaths of all his friends as well (Knowledge [history] DC 15). Driven nearly mad by the ghostly perceptions haunting him, Filix eventually fl ed society entirely, living out the rest of his long days alone in the wilderness (Knowledge [history] DC 20).

Blindhelm.description: This helm’s face is a solid slab of iron, apparently allowing its wearer no way to see out. Despite its appearance and name, a blindhelm doesn’t restrict your vision in any way. More important, it grants you blindsense out to 5 feet and provides you with a +5 competence bonus on saves against gaze attacks.

Executioner’s hoodR.description:
While worn, if you are chaotic evil, neutral evil, or chaotic neutral, you can attempt a coup de grace attack as a standard action rather than a full-round action.

Relic power (worship Erythnul and sacrifice a 5th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 9 HD): each successful coup de grace grants a +1 morale bonus on melee attack and damage rolls and 1 temporary hit point per level for 5 minutes (stacks to a maximum of +5 and 5 temporary hit points per level).

Fiendhelm.description:
Evil-aligned wearer only. Melee attacks are treated as evil-aligned for overcoming damage reduction. This is a continuous effect.

In addition, once per day each: dispel good, inflict critical wounds, and resist energy (electricity or fire only) as spell-like abilities.

Grants no benefit to nonevil wearers.

Lore gem.description:
Affixes to your forehead (standard action to attach or remove). While worn, gain a +2 competence bonus on Knowledge checks after spending a full round in contemplation (continuous effect, requires contemplation each time).

Also functions as a spellbook holding up to thirty spells of any levels. Recording a spell requires normal time and expense; erase an existing spell as a standard action.

Incarnum circlet.description:
Matches the Crown chakra (head slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Mindvault.description:
When activated, your consciousness transfers into the gem for 1 round. You are immune to mind-affecting spells and abilities but dazed for that round. You can activate after learning the result of an incoming mind-affecting effect but before it is applied (negating it entirely).

Functions once per day. Must wear for 24 hours before use; if removed, inactive until worn another 24 hours.

Platinum helmR.description:
While wearing it, immune to the frightful presence of evil dragons if you are lawful good, neutral good, or lawful neutral.

Relic power (worship Bahamut and sacrifice a 5th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 9 HD): when you make an attack, create a frightful presence effect. Each foe within 60 feet must succeed on a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your HD + Cha modifier) or be panicked (4 or fewer HD) or shaken (5+ HD) for 4d6 rounds. Functions three times per day.

With an 8th-level divine sacrifice or True Believer and 15 HD, once per day command the helm to grant blindsense 60 feet for 1 hour.

Watch lamp.description: This plain mithral headband has no distinguishing features. Wand bracelet When activated, a watch lamp creates a globe of light that hovers at your shoulder and sheds light as a torch. The light follows you wherever you move. It can be extinguished with a second command.

Eyes of Doom.description: Bestow curse, eyebite, ray of enfeeblement, symbol of pain.

Goggles of Minute Seeing.description: +5 competence bonus on Appraise and Search checks.

Crystal mask of detection.description: This crystal mask is held in place with black cloth straps, and it covers only the top portion of the face. Translucent and shot through with golden fl ecks, the mask is warm to the touch. A crystal mask of detection grants you a +10 competence bonus on Search checks.

Crystal mask of discernment.description: This transparent crystal mask has a pale green tinge and covers all the face, except the mouth and jaw. Its straps are made of silvery cloth. A crystal mask of discernment grants you a +10 insight bonus on Sense Motive checks.

Crystal mask of dread.description: This black crystal mask is mapped with writhing red veins. It hides the entire face, revealing only the eyes. Black leather straps hold it tightly in place. A crystal mask of dread grants you a +10 competence bonus on Intimidate checks.

Crystal mask of insight.description: A simple, thin band of turquoise crystal, this item masks only the eyes. Thin, bone-white twine holds it in place. A crystal mask of insightful detection grants you a +9 insight bonus on Search and Spot checks.

Crystal mask of knowledge.description: This mask features a piece of curved golden crystal that covers the forehead. The crystal is attached to the front of a dark brown cloth cap. A crystal mask of knowledge grants you a +5 competence bonus on a specifi c type of Knowledge check. For instance, one mask would add the bonus on Knowledge (psionics) checks, while another would do so for Knowledge (history).

Crystal mask of languages.description: This cobalt mask covers only the lower portion of the face. Light brown leather straps hold it in place. Each of the various crystal masks of languages grants you the ability to speak, read, and write fi ve different languages. For example, a mask might grant the ability to speak, read, and write Celestial, Draconic, Dwarven, Elven, and Infernal.

Crystal mask of mindarmor.description: The front portion of this periwinkle crystal mask drops down below the eyes. Narrow slits in the mask allow the wearer full range of vision. A crystal mask of mindarmor grants you a +4 insight bonus on Will saving throws.

Crystal mask of psionic craft.description: This bright blue opaque eye covering extends up to the hairline, down below the nose, and back to the ears. Though easy to pull off if desired, the mask fi ts snugly and needs no straps to keep it in place. A crystal mask of psionic craft grants you a +10 competence bonus on Psicraft checks.

Crystal mask of visual insight.description: When activated, allows you to ignore the effects of concealment (but not invisibility) on any creature within 30 feet for 1 round.

Dragon mask.description:
When activated, allows you to use see invisibility (as the spell) for 5 minutes.

Functions two times per day.

Fanged mask.description:
When activated, you can make a magic bite attack dealing 1d6 points of damage plus your Strength modifier (Medium). Bite damage does not stack with any bite attack you already have.

Three times per day, upon biting a target while activating the mask, you can force a DC 13 Fortitude save or the target is stunned for 1 round.

Goggles of day.description: When placed over your eyes, operate without penalty in preternaturally bright light (such as from a flare, sunbeam, or sunburst spell). Also allow a vampire wearer to take a full-round action prior to dissolution when confronted with sunlight, instead of just a move or standard action.

Goggles of draconic vision.description:
Grant a +5 bonus on Spot checks, low-light vision, and darkvision out to 60 feet. While wearing them, you cannot be blinded by the cloud a hovering dragon creates (the cloud still provides concealment). Continuous, no activation.

Once per day, activate to grant blindsense out to 30 feet for 1 minute.

Goggles of foefinding.description: When you attack foes that have cover, you can ignore the cover bonus to AC they would normally receive. Does not allow you to circumvent total cover.

Goggles of lifesight.description:
When activated, automatically know whether any visible creature within 30 feet is alive, dead, undead, or neither alive nor dead (such as a construct). Blocked by any effect that would prevent detect undead.

Functions three times per day.

Goggles of the ebon hunter.description: Grant darkvision out to 30 feet and a +1 competence bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls with ranged weapons.

Goggles of the golden sun.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Spot checks in bright light 1/day.

Lens of the desert.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Survival checks in desert terrain 1/day.

Mask of blood.description:
If wearing this mask when you enter a rage or frenzy, that ability lasts 1 round longer than normal (continuous, no activation).

When activated, spit a stream of blood as a ranged touch attack (30 feet) for 4d6 acid damage.

Functions two times per day.

Mask of lies.description:
While wearing this mask, gain a +5 competence bonus on Bluff checks and your alignment is disguised as if by undetectable alignment (continuous, no activation).

When activated, alter your appearance as if by disguise self.

Functions three times per day.

Mask of masterySP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on one skill 1/day.

Mask of mental armor.description: Gain a +3 resistance bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting spells and abilities.

Mask of silent trickery.description:
When activated, omit the verbal component of the next enchantment or illusion spell you cast before the end of your turn (as if applying Silent Spell, but without altering the spell's level or casting time).

Functions two times per day.

Mask of sweet air.description: While wearing this mask, breathe freely in air fouled by smoke, dust, and fumes without fear of suffocation. Gain a +5 bonus on saves against airborne stench attacks and inhaled poisons (such as troglodyte stench or cloudkill).

Mask of the tigerSP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Hide and Move Silently checks 1/day.

Moonstone mask.description: Highlights of moonstone accentuate every ridge and curve of this angular silver mask. A moonstone mask grants you a +5 competence bonus on Listen and Spot checks, as well as darkvision out to 30 feet.

Spellsight spectacles.description: These round-rimmed spectacles have bluetinted lenses and a clear gem set into the joint of each earpiece. Spellsight spectacles grant you a +5 competence bonus on Spellcraft checks made to decipher scrolls and Use Magic Device checks made to use scrolls.

Cannith goggles.description: These light orange, round, translucent lenses are mounted within a fl exible metal frame that has a soft leather strap with a clasp at the back. Cannith goggles grant a +5 competence bonus on Search and Spot checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, an artifi cer (ECS 29) can power the goggles with infusions, granting them greater power. Using an infusion to power Cannith goggles requires 10 minutes and the expenditure of an infusion of the appropriate level. An infusion powers the goggles for 24 hours. Cannith goggles powered with a 1st-level infusion grant low-light vision. Powering them with a 2nd-level infusion grants the wearer low-light vision and darkvision out to 60 feet. If powered with a 3rd-level infusion, the goggles provide low-light vision and darkvision out to 120 feet. Lore: The artifi cers of a powerful noble family called House Cannith were tasked to fi nd a way to enhance human vision, and the results of their research are Cannith goggles (Knowledge [history] DC 20).

Artificer’s monocle.description: Putting on the monocle is a standard action. While wearing it, whenever you successfully use your artificer knowledge class feature to detect an item's magical aura, or you cast detect magic and have at least 5 ranks of Knowledge (arcana), you can spend 1 additional minute studying the item to identify its abilities as if you had cast identify upon it.

Blighter’s hex-eye.description:
When activated, your melee attacks against elves and creatures of the plant type deal an extra 2d6 points of damage for 1 round.

Functions three times per day, but cannot be activated in consecutive rounds.

Blindfold of true darkness.description: While worn, you gain blindsight out to 30 feet. Because your eyes are protected, you are also immune to gaze attacks and spells or effects that rely on sight. You cannot use vision in any way while wearing the blindfold.

Corsair’s eye patch.description: A leather strap and a bit of hardened leather and krenshar hide make up this menacing black eyepatch. Despite its name, the improved visual acuity provided by a corsair’s eyepatch is equally useful to landlubbers. Wearing this eyepatch has no negative impact on your eyesight—it appears completely transparent to you. While you wear a corsair’s eyepatch over your left eye, activating it grants you the ability to use see invisibility (as the spell) for 1 round. While you wear it over your right eye, activating it grants you the benefi t of the Blind-Fight feat for 1 minute. Moving the eyepatch from one eye to another requires a move action that doesn’t provoke attacks of opportunity. A corsair’s eyepatch functions a total of three times per day.

Dreaming blindfold.description:
While worn, you are effectively blinded, but once per day you can send a dream or nightmare to a specific known living creature within 100 miles (as the dream or nightmare spell, but not both in the same day, and dream cannot be delivered through a messenger).

If also wearing a psicrown, you can target the recipient with a single psionic power from the psicrown as if adjacent (costs double power points and renders the blindfold inert for seven days).

Eyes of expanded vision.description: Flanking opponents gain only a +1 bonus when attacking you instead of +2 (you remain flanked). You gain a +2 competence bonus on Spot checks but take a -2 penalty on saves against gaze attacks. These are continuous effects.

Eyes of power leech.description: Once per day, drain power points from a psionic creature within 40 feet by meeting its gaze (DC 16 Will negates). Drains 1d6 power points per round you concentrate (maximum 7 rounds). You gain 1 power point per round drained (subject to your normal maximum). No effect on nonpsionic targets or creatures with 0 power points.

Eyes of power leech, vampiric.description: As eyes of power leech, except you gain power points even if doing so would bring you over your normal maximum. You can maintain concentration for up to 13 rounds. Power points gained in excess of your maximum fade after 8 hours if not spent.

Eyes of truth.description: This pair of thin, crystal lenses has a faint blue tint. Eyes of truth grant you improved visual acuity, and can allow you briefl y to see the world as it truly is. While worn, the lenses grant you a +5 competence bonus on Spot checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, you can activate the lenses to gain a true seeing effect (as the spell) for 1 round. This ability functions once per day.

Lenses of bright vision.description: These thin, yellow quartz lenses are the size of two coins. Lenses of bright vision allow you to project light from your eyes. When placed over your eyes (a standard action), these lenses meld into your face, and the irises of your eyes take on a faint yellow hue. When you activate the lenses, they create a 15-foot cone of bright light emanating from your eyes. Unlike normal light sources, the lenses produce no shadowy illumination. The illumination lasts for 10 minutes or until you expend another swift action to douse it. Treat this effect as a 0-level spell for the purpose of interacting with darkness effects. This ability functions three times per day.

Lenses of true form.description:
While wearing the lenses, gain a +10 circumstance bonus on Spot checks to see through a disguise (continuous, no activation).

Once per day, activate to force a single shapeshifted creature within 30 feet to resume its natural form (Will DC 14 negates). On failure it immediately assumes its natural form, shape, and size and cannot assume a different shape for 2d4 rounds.

Incarnum goggles.description:
Matches the Brow chakra (face slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Monocle of perusal.description: Highlights of moonstone accentuate every ridge and curve of this angular silver mask. A moonstone mask grants you a +5 competence bonus on Listen and Spot checks, as well as darkvision out to 30 feet.

Pearl of speech.description:
Place on your tongue (standard action); absorbed until you speak the release command. While absorbed, speak and understand one specific language (each pearl is created for a specific language; only one pearl of speech active at a time).

While active, once per day produce a command effect (Will DC 11 negates).

Snakeblood Tooth.description: This tiny chunk of ivory resembles the fang of a snake. When placed into an empty tooth socket, a snakeblood tooth adheres to your jawbone until removed. Placing or removing a tooth requires a standard action. A snakeblood tooth grants you a +5 alchemical bonus on saves against ingested poisons. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, a snakeblood tooth has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges enhances a tooth’s protective qualities as described below. 1 charge: You gain a +5 alchemical bonus on saves against all types of poison for 3 rounds. 2 charges: You and up to three allies within 10 feet of you gain a +5 alchemical bonus on saves against poison for 3 rounds. 3 charges: You gain immunity to poison for 1 round.

Veil Of Allure.description: Dyed in swirls of pink and purple, this semitransparent veil looks like the garb of a sultan’s harem dancer. A veil of allure increases the save DC of your enchantment spells or spell-like abilities, as well as the Charisma-based save DCs of your extraordinary or supernatural abilities, by 2.

Soulstone.description: This faceted gemstone is the size of a coin and mounted on a golden disk covered in runes and scrollwork. Soulstones are boons to all those who channel the power of incarnum (see Magic of Incarnum). When you use the proper command thought, this gem adheres to the center of your forehead (the same command thought causes the soulstone to disengage). While you wear a soulstone, you can bind a soulmeld to your brow chakra, gaining the normal ability granted by such a bind. This effect functions despite the fact that you are currently wearing a magic item in that chakra. This bind is in addition to any binds you are already allowed (though it doesn’t let you bind an extra soulmeld to your brow chakra). If you are already able to bind soulmelds to your brow chakra, the soulstone instead grants you 1 bonus essentia that can be invested only in a soulmeld bound to your brow chakra. If you also wear a magic item that grants an enhancement bonus to your Wisdom, a soulstone also allows you to unshape a soulmeld bound to your brow chakra and immediately reshape another soulmeld, even binding it to your brow chakra if you want. This ability requires a full-round action and functions once per day.

Amulet of Mighty Fists +1.description: +1 enhancement bonus on unarmed/natural attack and damage rolls.

Amulet of Mighty Fists +2.description: +2 enhancement bonus on unarmed/natural attack and damage rolls.

Amulet of Mighty Fists +3.description: +3 enhancement bonus on unarmed/natural attack and damage rolls.

Amulet of Mighty Fists +4.description: +4 enhancement bonus on unarmed/natural attack and damage rolls.

Amulet of Mighty Fists +5.description: +5 enhancement bonus on unarmed/natural attack and damage rolls.

Periapt of Health.description: Immune to all diseases, including supernatural.

Periapt of Wound Closure.description: Continuous stabilize; 10% chance auto-stabilize when dying.

Phylactery of Faithfulness.description: Alerts wearer when about to violate alignment.

Scarab of Protection.description: +2 saves vs. evil magic; absorbs destruction spells.

Scarab, Golembane.description: +2 dodge bonus to AC against golems.

Amber amulet of vermin, giant bee.description:
Summons a giant bee that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, giant praying ma.description:
Summons a giant praying mantis that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, giant queen ant.description:
Summons a giant queen ant that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, giant stag beetl.description:
Summons a giant stag beetle that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, giant wasp.description:
Summons a giant wasp that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, Huge monstrous c.description:
Summons a Huge monstrous centipede that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, Huge monstrous s.description:
Summons a Huge monstrous scorpion that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amber amulet of vermin, Large monstrous.description:
Summons a Large monstrous spider that appears and obeys your commands for 1 minute (as summon nature's ally). At the end of the duration, the creature vanishes.

Functions once per day.

Amulet of aquatic salvation.description:
When activated, grants the ability to breathe water (as the water breathing spell) for 5 rounds.

Functions once per day.

Amulet of catapsi.description:
When activated, emits a 30-foot-radius area of telepathic noise, centered on you, for 9 rounds. If any creature in this area (including you) attempts to manifest a power, it must succeed on a DC 17 Will save or pay 4 more power points than it otherwise would.

Functions once per day.

Amulet of emergency healing.description:
When activated, heals 1d4+5 points of damage to yourself or a creature within 30 feet. Can prevent death of a creature reduced below -9 hit points if the healing returns the target to -9 or higher. No effect on undead or creatures immune to healing.

Functions three times per day.

Amulet of fortune prevailing.description:
When activated, reroll a saving throw. You must activate before the success or failure of the saving throw has been determined, and you must use the second result, even if it is lower. You cannot use this if you have already rerolled the save for any reason.

Functions once per day.

Amulet of incarnum shielding.description: While worn, you are immune to any effect that would drain your incarnum or unshape one of your soulmelds. The amulet has 7 charges when created; each time it negates an effect, it expends 1 charge. When it loses its last charge, it crumbles into dust.

Amulet of inviolate form.description:
Protects you from spells and effects that would significantly change your bodily form or composition. Whenever you are the target of such an effect, you can expend 1 charge to negate the transformation (decide before attempting a saving throw). Includes polymorph, petrification, aboleth tentacle touch, chaos beast attacks, and similar effects.

Has 7 charges when created; each expended charge turns one garnet dull black.

Amulet of retributive healing.description:
When activated, the next effect you use before the end of your turn that heals another creature's damage also heals you of an equal amount, as long as you could be healed by that same effect. If the effect heals multiple creatures, you only gain the retributive healing once per effect.

Functions three times per day.

Amulet of second chances.description:
When activated, undo all the events of your current turn and begin the turn again. This resets positions, damage, conditions, and all other events of the turn. All affected creatures are aware of the now-nonexistent round but may make entirely different choices.

Functions once per day.

Amulet of teamwork.description:
When you successfully use the aid another action, the bonus granted to your ally improves from +2 to +3. When you flank an enemy, you and any allies also flanking that creature gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls. These are continuous effects.

In addition, once per day you can activate the amulet to grant both you and an adjacent ally a +5 competence bonus to AC for 1 round.

Amulet of tears.description: Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1, 2, or 3 charges when you activate the amulet grants 12, 18, or 24 temporary hit points for up to 10 minutes. These do not stack with other temporary hit points.

Amulet of toxin delay.description:
When activated, creates a delay poison effect on you (as the spell, but with a duration of only 2 rounds). You can activate whenever you would be required to attempt a saving throw against poison (before rolling the save).

Functions once per day.

Amulet of wordtwisting.description: Strips of torn and knotted scrolls seem to make up this amulet. You can discern a few letters in red ink on the tightly rolled parchment, but these symbols are warped and meaningless. An amulet of wordtwisting grants you a +2 insight bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. If you are an orc wearing an amulet of wordtwisting, you instead gain a +4 insight bonus on the indicated skill checks. In addition, once per day, you can activate a tongues effect on yourself (as the spell).

Choker of life protectionSP.description: Grants a +2 resistance bonus on saves against death effects 1/day.

Collar of healing.description:
Functions only when worn by your animal companion, familiar, or special mount. While worn, you always know the creature's exact hit point total.

You can activate the collar from any distance on the same plane to instantly heal the wearer of 50 points of damage and remove the fatigued or exhausted condition.

Functions once per day.

Dragon spirit amulet.description:
When activated, your draconic aura affects you and all allies within 60 feet to whom you have line of effect for 1 round.

Functions three times per day.

Dragon’s eye amulet.description: While worn, you gain a +10 competence bonus on Search and Spot checks, and blindsense out to 30 feet.

Enduring amulet.description: This circular, coin-shaped talisman is half fl ame red and half ice blue. An enduring amulet protects you from extremes of temperature, as if by the endure elements spell. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, the amulet has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges grants you resistance to cold and fi re for 1 round. 1 charge: Resistance to cold 10 and fi re 10. 2 charges: Resistance to cold 15 and fi re 15. 3 charges: Resistance to cold 20 and fi re 20.

Farspeaking amulet.description:
The wearer carries the central green crystal while up to four others carry removable red charms. When activated, creates a link enabling all affected characters on the same plane to converse as if standing together for up to 10 minutes.

Functions three times per day. If you also wear an item granting a competence bonus on Listen checks, you can listen to the environment of linked characters as if present.

Fireflower pendant.description:
While worn, you gain resistance to fire 10.

When activated, you gain immunity to fire until the start of your next turn. Doing this renders the pendant powerless for 1 hour.

Golembane scarab.description: Grants a +2 enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls against constructs 1/day.

Heartseeking amulet.description:
When activated, the next melee attack you make is made as a melee touch attack instead. If you don't make a melee attack by the end of your turn, the effect is wasted.

Functions three times per day.

Necklace of copper dragon scales.description: Six coin-sized copper dragon scales adorn this simple brown cord. Upon touching one of the six scales that make up this necklace and uttering a command word, you gain resistance to acid 5 for 1 hour. Once all six scales are used, the necklace becomes dull and loses its magic.

Necklace of warning.description: Warns you of impending danger, granting a +2 bonus to your flat-footed AC (up to a maximum value equal to your normal AC).

Pendant of joy.description: This teardrop-shaped amulet is made of bluegreen crystal. When it is worn by a character who has at least 1 power point, a pendant of joy subtly reaches out to nearby minds, touching them with feelings of pleasure and contentment. All creatures within 30 feet (including the wearer) gain a +2 morale bonus on Diplomacy checks.

Periapt of the sullen seaSP.description: Grants water breathing and a +5 competence bonus on Swim checks 1/day.

Retributive amulet.description: This inch-wide disk bears a large red crystal in its center. Tiny carvings around the crystal show an endless march of angels and demons at war. You can activate a retributive amulet immediately after another creature has dealt damage to you with a melee attack. That creature takes damage equal to half the damage it dealt to you. This damage is of the same type (or types). This ability functions three times per day.

Scarab of invulnerability.description: This broad adamantine necklace bears a scarab of the same metal, polished to a mirrorlike sheen. A scarab of invulnerability provides you with brief but complete protection from all forms of damage. For 1 round after activating the scarab, you are immune to all forms of damage (including ability damage), whether from weapons, spells, psionic powers, poison, environmental effects, or any other cause. The scarab doesn’t protect you from harmful effects that don’t deal damage. A scarab of invulnerability functions once per day. You must wear a scarab of invulnerability for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours.

Scarab of stabilization.description: This solid gold scarab has obsidian chips for eyes. Glyphs of protection and health are carved into its carapace. A button at the base of its abdomen makes its legs clench or release, allowing it to be worn as a cloak clasp or a decorative piece of jewelry. If your hit points drop below 0 but you are still alive (above –10 hit points), a scarab of stabilization automatically stabilizes you. You are still unconscious from your wounds, but you need not make a stabilization check each round to prevent further hit point loss. If another wound causes your hit points to drop lower, the scarab automatically stabilizes you again at your new hit point total, provided that it is still between –1 and –9. If a wound would drop you to –10 hit points or lower, the secondary power of the scarab activates automatically. You do not die, but your hit point total becomes –1, and you automatically stabilize. This power functions only once—the scarab crumbles into dust as soon as this ability is used.

Spellsink scarab.description: Exotic and arcane symbols circumscribe this scarab-shaped brooch. You can activate a spellsink scarab whenever you would take hit point damage from a spell or psionic power. A scarab has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges reduces the damage dealt to you by the spell or power (to a minimum of 0 points of damage). 1 charge: Reduce damage by 2d6 points. 2 charge: Reduce damage by 3d6 points. 3 charges: Reduce damage by 4d6 points.

Torc of displacement.description: This glass torc is partially silvered. From some angles it is perfectly transparent; from others it refl ects like a mirror. A torc of displacement allows you to temporarily shift your image slightly away from your current location. A torc has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges grants you concealment for 1 round. The effectiveness of this concealment (the miss chance provided) varies depending on the number of charges spent. 1 charge: 20% miss chance. 2 charges: 30% miss chance. 3 charges: 40% miss chance.

Torc of heroic sacrifice.description: This jet-black torc is crafted in the form of two clasped hands. A torc of heroic sacrifi ce allows you to take an injury meant for your ally. When you activate this torc, you take all the damage dealt to a single ally by a single attack or effect that has just occurred. The damage dealt to you has no type, and therefore ignores any energy resistance, damage reduction, or immunities you might have. The ally must be within 30 feet, and you must have line of sight to that ally. This ability functions once per day.

Torc of power preservation.description: This copper neck band is inlaid with gold, silver, and platinum. The inlays take the form of a dragon writhing across the surface of the torc. Five times per day, you can manifest a power by paying power points equal to the standard cost minus 1 (minimum 1).

Torc of the titans.description: Dramatic, but simple, images of clouds and lightning blots cover this thick, golden neck ring. When you activate a torc of the titans, you gain a +5 morale bonus on Strength checks, Strength-based skill checks, and melee weapon damage rolls for 1 round. This ability functions three times per day.

Unicorn pendant.description: A marble unicorn’s head with a horn of gold has been sculpted onto the face of this pendant. A unicorn pendant has two abilities, each of which can be activated once per day: cure moderate wounds (2d8+10 points of damage) and neutralize poison. In addition, if you are a paladin wearing a unicorn pendant, you treat your Charisma as 4 points higher than normal for the purpose of your lay on hands ability. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation.

Vampire torc.description: Tunic of steady spellcasting This black iron torc bears a ruby that seems to weep tears of blood. A vampire torc allows you to heal your own wounds when you deal damage to an enemy. When you activate this torc, the next successful melee attack you make before the end of your turn also heals you of an amount of damage equal to half the damage your attack dealt. This ability functions equally well regardless of your creature type. If you don’t deal damage with a melee attack before the end of your turn, that activation of the torc is wasted. This ability functions two times per day.

Wyrmfang amulet.description: This necklace consists of a leather thong strung with dragon’s teeth. All attacks you make with natural weapons or unarmed strikes while wearing this amulet overcome damage reduction as through they were magic weapons. (The attacks don’t gain an enhancement bonus, just the ability to overcome some creatures’ damage reduction.)

Ankh of ascension.description:
When activated, you must sacrifice a prepared divine spell or divine spell slot. The caster level of any divine spells of that level or lower that you cast before your next turn increases by 4.

Functions three times per day.

Badge of the svirfneblin.description:
While worn, you gain darkvision out to 30 feet and a +5 competence bonus on Hide checks. These are continuous effects.

Once per day, you can activate the badge to give yourself a blur effect (as the spell).

Brooch of avoidance.description:
When activated, grants a +4 dodge bonus against attacks of opportunity until the end of your turn.

Functions three times per day.

Brooch of stability.description:
When your hit points are reduced to -1 or lower, you automatically become stable (assuming the damage was not enough to kill you).

Functions once per day.

Cat’s-eye brooch.description: Provides a +1 luck bonus on all saving throws and an additional +4 bonus on saving throws against disease.

Charm of countersong.description: Once per day, you can activate your countersong bardic music ability as an immediate action to interrupt a sonic attack.

Chasuble of fell power.description: While worn, your eldritch blasts or spells you cast that have the chaotic descriptor deal an extra 1d6 points of damage. Only a chaotic spell that deals hit point damage can benefit.

Chasuble of fell power, greater.description: As a chasuble of fell power, except the greater chasuble adds an extra 2d6 points of damage to your eldritch blasts or damaging chaotic-descriptor spells you cast.

Chronocharm of the celestial wanderer.description:
When activated, you can immediately reroll a single Listen or Spot check that you just attempted. The result of the second roll replaces the first.

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Chronocharm of the fateweaver.description:
When activated, you can immediately reroll a single Balance, Climb, or Tumble check that you just attempted. The result of the second roll replaces the first.

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Chronocharm of the grand master.description:
When activated, you gain a +5 dodge bonus to your AC against a single ranged attack.

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Chronocharm of the horizon walker.description:
When activated, you can move up to half your speed as part of the same swift action used to activate it; this movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity (round down to nearest 5-foot increment).

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Chronocharm of the laughing rogue.description:
When activated, you can immediately reroll a single Disable Device check that you just attempted. You can activate after success or failure is determined (even if you set off a trap), but before ramifications are applied. The second roll replaces the first.

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Chronocharm of the uncaring archmage.description:
When activated, the next spell of 3rd level or lower you cast that has a casting time of 1 full round can be cast as a standard action instead. If you do not cast such a spell by the end of your turn, the effect fades.

Functions once per day.

You must wear a chronocharm for 24 hours before you can access its abilities. If it is taken off, it becomes inactive until worn for an additional 24 hours. Multiple different chronocharms can be worn simultaneously.

Contact medallion.description:
When activated, forges a telepathic bond (as Rary's telepathic bond) with one person within 1 mile who is known to you. The bond lasts for 1 minute.

Functions three times per day.

Crest of bravery.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges enhances your aura of courage for 5 rounds.

1 charge: The morale bonus granted by your aura of courage increases by 4.
2 charges: Your aura of courage affects all allies within 60 feet.
3 charges: Both of the above effects.

Eagle claw talisman.description:
When activated, you gain a +5 competence bonus on the next Strength check (or unarmed strike damage roll) you make to break or burst an item. This effect lasts for 1 round or until used.

Functions three times per day.

Empowered spellshard, 1st-level spell.description:
Keyed to a specific 1st-level spell. When you cast the attuned spell, you can use this shard as a focus to empower it (as the Empower Spell feat, with no adjustment to spell level or casting time).

Functions three times per day.

Empowered spellshard, 2nd-level spell.description:
Keyed to a specific 2nd-level spell. When you cast the attuned spell, you can use this shard as a focus to empower it (as the Empower Spell feat, with no adjustment to spell level or casting time).

Functions three times per day.

Empowered spellshard, 3rd-level spell.description:
Keyed to a specific 3rd-level spell. When you cast the attuned spell, you can use this shard as a focus to empower it (as the Empower Spell feat, with no adjustment to spell level or casting time).

Functions three times per day.

Enemy spirit pouch.description: A small collection of teeth, small scales, knots of hair, and strange bits of jerky fi lls this pouch. The worn leather bag’s strings are long enough to allow the pouch to be worn as a necklace. This item is keyed to a single type of creature (aberration, animal, magical beast, and so on). A pouch keyed to humanoids or outsiders must be keyed to a subtype as described in the ranger’s favored enemy ability (PH 47). While wearing an enemy spirit pouch, you gain a +1 competence bonus on attack rolls against creatures of this type. If you are a ranger, the pouch increases your favored enemy bonus against creatures of its type by 2. If you don’t have the favored enemy ability, the pouch grants you a +2 bonus on damage rolls and certain skill checks as if that type of creature were your favored enemy. If a creature of the type keyed to the pouch wears that pouch, it receives one negative level. The negative level remains for as long as the pouch is worn and disappears when it is no longer carried. The negative level never results in actual level loss, but cannot be overcome in any way (including restoration spells) while the pouch is worn.

Flesh ring of scorn.description:
Must be worn as a piercing through the flesh of your upper chest. When you score a critical threat, you can activate the ring to automatically confirm the threat, but the ring deals damage to you based on your weapon's critical multiplier (×2: 2d6, ×3: 3d6, ×4: 4d6).

Functions three times per day.

Gem of the glitterdepthR.description: This black, thousand-faceted gem seems to sparkle with an inner fi re regardless of the ambient light. To gnomes, these gems are the very embodiment of the earth’s blessings. When you wear a gem of the glitterdepth, you gain a +2 enhancement bonus to Wisdom if you are neutral good, lawful good, chaotic good, or neutral. Relic Power: If you have established the proper divine connection, you also have blindsense out to 30 feet while wearing the gem. In addition, you know instinctively where the walls, ceiling, and fl oor are in any cavern you’re in, no matter how large or what the illumination. To use the relic power, you must worship Garl Glittergold and either sacrifice a 5th-level divine spell slot or have the True Believer feat and at least 9 HD. Lore: According to legend, Moradin wrested the fi ve original gems of the glitterdepth from the earth and gave them to Garl Glittergold, who cut and polished them and then hid them for his dedicated clergy to fi nd (Knowledge [religion] DC 20).

Githborn talisman.description:
When activated, gain a +2 insight bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against aberrations, and a +2 insight bonus on Will saves against aberration extraordinary, supernatural, and spell-like abilities. Lasts 1 round. Githyanki or githzerai improve these bonuses to +4.

Functions three times per day.

Incarnum amulet.description:
Matches the Throat chakra (neck slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Hand of the oak father.description: Six abilities, each once per day: barkskin, entangle, goodberry, plant growth, speak with plants, and tree shape (as the spells). The hand has hardness 10 and resistance 10 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and sonic.

Medal of gallantry.description: This silver brooch is shaped like the sun. A golden cabochon adorns the center. A medal of gallantry grants you a +2 bonus on Diplomacy checks but imposes a –2 penalty on Bluff checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. Three times per day, you can activate the medal to use sanctuary on yourself. If you attack while protected by this effect, you take a –1 morale penalty on that attack and all subsequent attacks for 1 hour afterward.

Psionatrix.description: Each discipline has its own psionatrix. Wearing one adds a +1 enhancement bonus to the save DC of powers of the appropriate discipline (e.g., telepathy psionatrix increases DC of psionic suggestion). Crystal glow varies by discipline: light blue (clairsentience), green (metacreativity), red (psychokinesis), violet (psychometabolism), orange (psychoportation), yellow (telepathy).

Reins of ascension.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges grants your mount:

1 charge: +10 competence bonus on Jump checks for 10 rounds.
2 charges: fly at normal land speed (average maneuverability) for 5 rounds.
3 charges: fly at double normal land speed (perfect maneuverability) for 1 round.

Reliquary holy symbol.description:
Functions as a holy symbol for a specific deity. For each prerequisite you meet, grants one additional daily use of turn or rebuke undead:
• At least 5 ranks in Knowledge (religion)
• Improved Turning feat
• At least one divine feat

Only useful to characters dedicated to that deity (or, for nondedicated characters, whose alignment matches the deity).

Safewing Emblem.description: This porcelain pin is sculpted to look like a pair of outstretched white wings. A tiny green stone adorns the center, where the wings meet. If you fall at least 10 feet, a safewing emblem becomes a pair of feathery wings that grant you a feather fall effect, allowing you to descend safely from any height up to 180 feet. When you land, the emblem shatters, its magic expended.

Scentblinder.description: This battered pouch of rough burlap dangles from a tattered cord. A strong aroma of herbs and vegetable decay emanates through the fabric. A scentblinder masks your odor, making the scent ability ineffective against you. Creatures cannot track you or detect your location by scent. It also masks the effect of extraordinary, spell-like, and supernatural abilities that you possess that are based on scent, such as a troglodyte’s stench.

Skull Plaque.description: The image of a grinning skull is engraved upon this ivory amulet. Black enamel inlays form a shield-shaped background. Undead creatures take a –2 penalty on attack rolls against the wearer of a skull plaque. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. A skull plaque also has two additional abilities, each of which can be activated once per day: death knell and detect undead.

Surge Crystal.description:
This leather band holds an indigo crystal that pulses with vibrant radiance. Has 5 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges increases your manifester level for the next psionic power you manifest that round (allowing higher augmentation; you must still pay the power point cost).

1 charge: +1 manifester level.
2 charges: +2.
3 charges: +3.
4 charges: +4.
5 charges: +5.

Wink Brooch.description: The ruby set at the head of this straight pin twinkles like the eye of a gnome with a secret. Many a diplomat wears a wink brooch to court to gain an edge in conversation. When activated, this brooch grants you a +2 competence bonus on a single Bluff or Diplomacy check attempted before the end of your turn. This ability functions three times per day.

Symbol of transfiguration.description: This holy symbol is warm and radiates a sense of peace and security, as if an angel had been wearing it close to her heart. Many good-aligned religions have some especially well-wrought holy symbols that are actually symbols of transfi guration. Three times per day, a symbol of transfi guration allows you to produce a purify food and drink effect. Once per day, you can use align weapon (good only) on any weapon you hold, though the duration is only 1 round.

Cloak of Arachnida.description: +8 competence bonus on Hide and Climb checks in webs; spider climb.

Cloak of Elvenkind.description: +5 competence bonus on Hide checks in natural surroundings.

Cloak of Resistance +1.description: +1 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Cloak of Resistance +2.description: +2 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Cloak of Resistance +3.description: +3 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Cloak of Resistance +4.description: +4 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Cloak of Resistance +5.description: +5 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Cloak of the Bat.description: Fly and polymorph self into bat.

Cloak of the Manta Ray.description: Swim speed 60 ft.; breathe water; freedom in water.

Mantle of Faith.description: SR 25 vs. evil spells and spell-like abilities.

Cape of the viper.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges transforms you into a viper for 7 rounds.

1 charge: Small viper.
2 charges: Medium viper.
3 charges: Large viper; add +2 to the save DC of your bite's poison.

While in viper form, you replace your statistics with those of a typical viper of the appropriate size, except alignment, hit points, Hit Dice, and ability to understand (but not speak) your languages. Gear melds into the new form. Treat as a polymorph effect.

Cloak of battle.description: Thousands of thin, silvery chain links make up this long cloak. A cloak of battle grants you a +4 armor bonus to AC. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. The cloak also has two other abilities tied to unique command words. The fi rst command transforms the cloak into an iron +1/+1 quarterstaff that leaps into your hands (or falls at your feet if you don’t have an open hand). The same command transforms the staff back into a cloak, returning it to your shoulders. The second command allows the cloak to make a disarm attempt against an adjacent opponent (using your base attack bonus and size modifi er, and with a +4 bonus on the roll). This attempt does not provoke attacks of opportunity. If the disarm attempt succeeds, the cloak can fl ing the weapon up to 10 feet in any direction. (The disarmed weapon can’t be thrown at a creature as an attack.) If the disarm attempt fails, you can’t be disarmed in return. This ability can be used three times per day.

Cloak of elemental protection.description:
When activated, you gain resistance 10 against a single energy type of your choice (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic) until the start of your next turn.

Functions once per day. You must wear the cloak for 24 hours before you can access its abilities; if removed, it becomes inactive until worn for another 24 hours.

Cloak of predatory vigor.description:
While in a rage or frenzy, you can activate this cloak to heal damage to yourself equal to your Hit Dice.

Functions two times per day.

Cloak of quills.description:
While grappling, each time you roll a grapple check (other than the initial check to enter or resist the grapple), you can activate the cloak to lodge quills in your opponent, dealing 1d6 points of damage to each other creature in the grapple (Reflex DC 16 negates).

Functions five times per day.

Cloak of soulbound resistance.description: This fi nely crafted garment is made of blue and purple material. A pair of matched blue quartz crystals form the cloak’s clasp. A cloak of soulbound resistance grants you a +1 resistance bonus on your saving throws. In addition, it serves as a receptacle for your essentia, much as a soulmeld does. Every point of essentia invested in it increases the bonus by 1, subject to the maximum values noted below. Each cloak of soulbound resistance has an essentia capacity. The maximum number of essentia points that can be invested in it equals this value or your normal essentia capacity (see Table 2–1: Essentia Capacity, Magic of Incarnum 19), whichever is lower. Investing or reallocating essentia in the cloak is a swift action (and can be done as part of the normal swift action used for reallocating essentia). A cloak of soulbound resistance has a maximum essentia capacity of 2, and the maximum resistance bonus it can grant is +3. Chakra Bind (Shoulders): A cloak of soulbound resistance improves your resistance bonus by an additional 1 when it is bound to your shoulders chakra, which can exceed the maximum values described above. See Binding Items to Chakras (MoI 108) for more information.

Cloak of soulbound resistance, greater.description: As a cloak of soulbound resistance, except maximum essentia capacity 4 and maximum resistance bonus +5.

Cloak of stone.description: This mottled gray cloak looks like an incredibly thin sheet of stone. While wearing a cloak of stone, you gain a +5 competence bonus on Hide and Move Silently checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, you can activate the cloak to merge with stone as though using the meld into stone spell. This ability functions once per day.

Cloak of the salamander.description:
Flames dance over the cloak's surface but do not harm you or creatures or objects you touch. When activated, any creature that strikes you in melee with a natural weapon or nonreach manufactured weapon takes 1d6+7 points of fire damage. This effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn.

Functions three times per day.

Cloak of the salamander, greater.description: As a cloak of the salamander, except the fire damage effect against melee attackers is continuous and requires no activation.

Cloak of thorns.description: This brown cloak seems to be composed entirely of long thorns. A cloak of thorns grants you a +2 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor bonus. (A creature without natural armor has an effective natural armor bonus of +0.) Cloak of the salamander In addition, any creature striking you in melee with a natural weapon or nonreach manufactured weapon takes 1d4+3 points of piercing damage.

Cloak of turn resistance.description: This cloak is little more than a tattered piece of homespun cloth, secured by a rotted leather thong at the neck. While you wear this cloak, your turn resistance increases by 4.

Cloak of weaponry.description: Has an extradimensional space that can hold a single weapon weighing up to 25 pounds and no larger than Medium. When activated, you can draw forth the weapon or stow a weapon if the cloak is empty. Creatures and nonweapon objects cannot be stored.

Mantle of second chances.description: Once per day, reroll one roll you have just made before the DM declares whether it succeeded or failed. You must accept the reroll result, even if worse. Works even if you already have a similar ability (such as the Luck domain granted power).

Mantle of the predatorSP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Hide and Move Silently checks 1/day.

Piercer cloak.description:
When activated, any attack you make on your current turn from higher ground deals an extra 1d6 damage. If you charge from at least 10 feet above the enemy, extra damage increases to 2d6. Lasts 1 round.

Functions three times per day.

Rearguard’s cape.description:
If you are a marshal and visible active enemies within 60 feet exceed your visible active allies (including yourself) within 60 feet, activate this cape. While active, the bonus granted by your minor and major auras improves by 2. Lasts 10 rounds or until you and your allies are no longer outnumbered.

Functions once per day.

Transposer cloak.description: This cloak is divided diagonally into two colors. The upper portion is pure white, while the lower is black as night. A transposer cloak allows you to switch positions with another creature. When you activate this cloak, you and any one creature within 30 feet swap positions. Both you and the target must occupy the same size of space on the battlefi eld (for instance, a human could swap places with a dwarf or a goblin, but not with an ogre or a cat). If the other creature is unwilling to trade positions, it is allowed a DC 13 Will save to negate the effect. Each creature can bring along objects weighing up to its maximum load, but additional creatures can’t be brought along. This ability functions three times per day.

Vanisher cloak.description: This silk cloak is seamless—a single sheet of gossamer, gray fabric. A vanisher cloak allows you and nearby allies to briefl y disappear from sight. A cloak has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges turns you (and perhaps one or more allies) invisible, as the invisibility spell, for 1 or more rounds. 1 charge: You become invisible for 4 rounds. 2 charges: You and one adjacent ally become invisible for 3 rounds. 3 charges: You and up to three adjacent allies become invisible for 2 rounds.

War wizard cloak.description: This long black cloak has a high collar and is cut to overlap a wearer’s chest and arms. An open white palm in a circle adorns the right breast, and a rampant purple dragon rages on the left. A war wizard cloak protects you from the elements (as endure elements) and also activates a feather fall effect on you automatically if you fall more than 5 feet. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, this cloak has four other abilities, each of which can be activated once per day: dimension door, mage armor, protection from arrows, and sending

Water cloak.description: This sailcloth cloak’s elaborate embroidery evokes the waves of the sea. Within the pattern of the waves, a repeating swirl of thread matches the symbol writhing within the large, dark crystal shard that forms the clasp at the neck of the cloak. The cloak appears damp, and the lower edge looks as though it has been soaked in water. Despite its damp appearance, a water cloak always feels completely dry to its wearer, even in pouring rain. This cloak grants you a +5 bonus on Refl ex saves against any effect that deals fi re damage and allows you to avoid fi re damage as if you had evasion. Whenever you make a successful Refl ex saving throw against any fi re effect that deals half damage on a successful save, you instead take no damage. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, a water cloak offers two other activated abilities. Extinguish: The cloak extinguishes all nonmagical fi res within 30 feet. This ability has no effect on fi re creatures. It functions fi ve times per day. Counterfi re: The cloak can counter any spell with the fi re descriptor (as if you were casting dispel magic). This ability functions once per day. Attempting to counter a spell that doesn’t have the fi re descriptor wastes the effect.

White cloak of the spider.description: This white cloak looks like it was woven by a gigantic arachnid. A large gray spider is embroidered upon the back amid radiating patterns of webbing. You gain a +2 bonus on saves against poison. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, this cloak has two other abilities, each of which can be activated once per day: spider climb and hold person.

Dragonscale cloakR.description:
While worn, you have resistance 5 to an energy type of your choice (acid, cold, electricity, or fire) if you are chaotic evil, neutral evil, or chaotic neutral. Select or change the resistance with a command word.

Relic power (worship Tiamat and sacrifice a 6th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 11 HD): transform the cloak into dragon wings for up to 2 hours per day (fly 60 ft., average maneuverability). Relic power also increases energy resistance to 20.

Incarnum cloak.description:
Matches the Shoulders chakra (shoulders slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Greater piwafwi.description: Formed of fi ne gray cloth smoother and lighter than silk, this delicate cloak is covered in a fi ne layer of spider webs. A greater piwafwi grants you resistance to fi re 5 and a +5 competence bonus on Hide checks. A lesser version of this item is equivalent to a cloak of elvenkind (DMG 253). Lore: The drow created piwafwis to conceal their scouts and warriors from the eyes of enemies. They claim that the cloaks of elvenkind crafted by their surface cousins are mere copies of these items (Knowledge [arcana] or Knowledge [dungeoneering] DC 15).

Scry shroud.description: The black lining of this nondescript gray cloak seems to absorb both light and sound. While wearing a scry shroud, you gain a +5 competence bonus on saves against divination spells. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. Additionally, if you see or otherwise detect a magical sensor created by a scrying spell, you can activate the cloak to become invisible (as the invisibility spell) for 10 rounds.

Robe of Eyes.description: +10 competence bonus on Search and Spot checks.

Robe of Scintillating Colors.description: Dazzles viewers; blindness and stunning effects.

Robe of Stars.description: Magic missile and other star-themed spell effects.

Robe of the Archmagi.description: +5 armor bonus, +8 resistance bonus on saves vs. spells, +4 spell DC.

Robe of arcane might.description: Mystical sigils line the hems of this bulky, thick-shouldered robe. Minuscule gems adorn the cuffs, collar, and hems. A robe of arcane might grants you a +4 armor bonus to AC. In addition, each robe is attuned to one of the eight schools of magic, chosen during creation (and identifi able with a successful DC 20 Spellcraft check). When casting a spell from that school, you gain a +1 competence bonus to your caster level.

Robe of mysterious conjuration.description: This robe seems to have depth, as if you were gazing into a portal between worlds, where vague behemoths move at the limit of your vision. A robe of mysterious conjuration intensifi es your connection to other planes, linking your mind to the distant realms from which a puissant mage can pluck servants and bind them to his will. Three times per day, you can activate the robe and sacrifi ce a prepared arcane spell (or arcane spell slot) to summon one or more monsters, as if you had cast a summon monster spell of the same level as the sacrifi ced spell from your class spell list. The summoned monsters can act immediately.

Robe of retaliation.description: An image of a solar eclipse with a blazing corona can be seen on the back of this heavy red robe. A robe of retaliation allows you to react to attacks with a burst of magical power. If you are struck by a creature wielding a melee weapon or a natural weapon, you can activate the robe and sacrifi ce an arcane spell of 1st level or higher to deal damage to your attacker equal to 1d6 points per level of the spell sacrifi ced. This ability functions three times per day.

Robe of the inferno.description: The coal-colored cloth of this robe is decorated with crimson-stitched runes. The runes run around the bottom and up the length of the garment in meandering lines, and are a mixture of arcane formulas and praises to fi re written in Ignan. The robe emits a faint odor of brimstone, and a gentle warmth surrounds it. While you wear this robe, it provides you with a +4 armor bonus to AC. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. The robe can also be activated as a swift (command) action by speaking a word in Ignan that causes four fi ery orbs, each about 6 inches in diameter, to spring from the sleeves. They circle slowly around your body at waist height as long as the robe remains active, but don’t interfere with your actions in any way. As long as they are active, they collectively provide light equal to that of a torch and grant you resistance to fi re 10. Two times per day, you can send one of the fi ery orbs streaking toward a single enemy within 60 feet as a ranged touch attack, which is a standard (command) action. Failure means the orb misses and explodes harmlessly in a shower of sparks. Success means it explodes in a 5-footradius burst around the target, dealing 8d6 points of fi re damage and leaving affected creatures dazzled for 1 round (Refl ex DC 16 halves the damage and negates the dazzled condition). Lore: The fi rst robe of the inferno was crafted by an elf evoker named Killaith Marcaun, who was badly burned in a battle against a large number of summoned thoqqua. Deeply humiliated by the scars Robe of mysterious conjuration Robe of the inferno Robe of retaliation

Robe of thunderSP.description: Deals 3d6 sonic damage to attackers who hit the wearer in melee 1/day.

Ghost shroud.description: This shroud looks like a ghostly, translucent version of a winding cloth used to wrap a body for burial. While wearing a ghost shroud, you gain a +1 defl ection bonus to Armor Class. In addition, all your melee attacks can affect incorporeal creatures as if you were wielding a ghost touch weapon.

Incarnum robe.description:
Matches the Soul chakra (body slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Shadow Veil.description: This greenish, indistinct robe is designed to envelop the entire body. A shadow veil is crafted from the essence of the undead creatures known as shadows. While wearing it, you gain a +1 defl ection bonus to AC. In addition, while in shadowy areas, you are treated as if you had concealment against creatures lacking darkvision, blindsight, or some other sensory ability that does not rely on light.

Shroud Of Scales.description: This opaque, reticulated shawl appears to contain hundreds of polychromatic dragon scales stitched into its fi bers. Deep, fl owing sleeves hang from long arm slits cut into the main body of the shawl. While wearing a shroud of scales, you can use your arms and hands normally and see through the garment despite its apparent opacity. You gain damage reduction 5/magic and are immune to a dragon’s frightful presence.

Vest of Escape.description: +30 competence bonus on Escape Artist checks.

Vest of Resistance +1.description: +1 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Vest of Resistance +2.description: +2 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Vest of Resistance +3.description: +3 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Vest of Resistance +4.description: +4 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Vest of Resistance +5.description: +5 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Vestment, Druid's.description: Extra wild shape and nature abilities for druids.

Bolt shirt.description:
You can teleport (with no chance of error) to any location within 60 feet, as long as you have both line of sight and line of effect. A harmless trail of crackling sparks traces your path. You cannot teleport into a space occupied by another creature or a solid object.

Functions once per day.

Chain shirt.description: Light armor providing +4 AC bonus with moderate armor check penalty.

Chain shirt, masterwork.description: Light armor providing +4 AC with masterwork quality.

Formless vest.description: Once per day, you can activate this vest to take half of the damage dealt to you by a critical hit or sneak attack. You can activate after damage has been determined, but before it has been dealt.

Hair shirt of suffering.description:
Provides a +1 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor (creatures without natural armor have an effective bonus of +0). Continuous, no activation.

Once per day, activate to use cure serious wounds on any creature other than yourself (healing 3d8+9 points of damage).

Mithralmist shirt.description: +1 mithral shirt, 7/day concealment against attack for 1 minute

Rogue’s vest.description: This slick-looking black vest is made of supple, well-worked leather. Its buttons are carved of dull black glass and seem to absorb light rather than refl ect it. While wearing this vest, you gain a +2 competence bonus on Hide and Move Silently checks, and a +2 competence bonus on Refl ex saves. In addition, if you have the skirmish, sneak attack, or sudden strike ability, you deal an extra 1d6 points of damage when making such an attack. Lore: A halfl ing arcane trickster named Lena Wanderingeye created the fi rst rogue’s vest. She wore it for many years before she retired from adventuring and joined a caravan. She eventually passed the secret of its creation on to the arcane spellcasters in the caravan (Knowledge [arcana or history] DC 20).

Sepulchral vest.description: The smell of fresh graves and decaying lilies clings to this vest. Sometimes jokingly dubbed “gravedigger’s shirts,” these items help characters who battle undead. When activated, a sepulchral vest grants you a +5 sacred bonus on saves against any effect generated by an undead creature for 1 round. This ability functions three times per day.

Shirt of angels.description: This shirt is composed entirely of soft, white feathers like those from an angel’s wings. Despite its composition, a shirt of angels is as durable as the sturdiest homespun. This item grants you damage reduction 3/evil.

Shirt of bone.description: This sturdy cotton shirt has bone stays that reinforce its shape. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/bludgeoning.

Shirt of chains.description: This silken shirt is looped with lengths of fi ne silver chain in an intricate pattern. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/piercing.

Shirt of demonskin.description: This leathery shirt is composed of bits of demonskin sewn together with a rough stitch. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/good.
