Shirt of ironskin.description: This shirt is made of interlocking iron plates. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/adamantine.

Shirt of resilience.description: This sturdy leather shirt is laced with a green leather cord. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/magic.

Shirt of slaadskin.description: This lightweight, brightly colored shirt is made from a rubbery, pebbled hide. This shirt is made from the skin of a slaad. It grants you damage reduction 3/lawful.

Shirt of the fey.description: This light blue shirt is made of material so fi ne it seems like gossamer. Embroidery along the neckline and cuffs depicts leaves and fl owers. The faint scent of grass envelops it. When donned, a shirt of the fey fades away, becoming almost impossible to see. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/cold iron.

Shirt of the inevitable.description: This metal sheath covers the chest and shoulders Shirt of angels Shirt of bone Shirt of demonskin This shirt is actually the metallic chestplate from one of the constructs of Mechanus—a noble inevitable. It grants you damage reduction 3/chaotic.

Shirt of the leech.description: This blood-red, silk shirt looks as though it was tailored to be form-fi tting. Whenever a healing spell of 4th level or lower is cast within 30 feet of you, you instantly recognize the spell being cast. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. As an immediate action, you can activate a shirt of the leech to gain the effect of any healing spell cast within 30 feet of you, instead of the spell affecting its intended target. If the spell would normally affect more than one creature, you choose which target does not gain the effect. You must have line of sight to both the caster and the target to use this effect. This ability functions three times per day.

Shirt of the moon.description: This shirt is made of gleaming, silvery material that feels like cool silk to the touch. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/silver.

Shirt of the treant.description: This vest is made of woven leaves and laced with a strong vine. The leaves that make up the fabric of this shirt were gathered from living treants. This shirt grants you damage reduction 3/slashing.

Shirt of wraith stalkingSP.description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Hide checks 1/day.

Vest of defense.description: This snug-fi tting vest is crafted from the skin of a huge tan-and-black snake. A vest of defense improves your ability to dodge attacks. When activated, this vest improves the dodge bonus to AC granted by fi ghting defensively, the total defense action, or the Combat Expertise feat by 2. Using more than one of these techniques doesn’t provide more than a +2 bonus. This bonus lasts for 1 round. This ability functions three times per day.

Vest of the archmagi.description: This gold-brocaded, burgundy garment seems fi t to be worn by the gods themselves. The vest of the archmagi is legendary. It fi lls the dreams of every apprentice sent to clean the inkpots and dust the tomes, but rumors of this item are unfounded. Whispering in the bunkrooms of the Academy at night, after the oil lamps are quenched and curfew has long passed, eager apprentices claim that a vest of the archmagi provides its wearer with continuous defensive bonuses equivalent to the most potent bracers of armor (+8 armor bonus to AC) and cloak of resistance (+5 resistance bonus on saving throws). It is also said that the vest provides its wearer with the continuous ability to overcome the magical defenses of her enemies (+2 enhancement bonus on caster level checks made to overcome spell resistance), and that it allows him to recall up to three arcane spells that he had previously prepared and cast (as a pearl of power, except that it can be used for any spell up to 9th level and activating it requires only a swift action). Finally, it is suggested, the wearer of the vest of the archmagi could expend a prepared arcane spell or spell slot to heal himself of damage equal to fi ve times the Unicorn pendant

Vest of the master evoker.description: This black silk vest is heavy with moonstone and jacinth beadwork sewn into arcane patterns on the back. Crackling with power, a vest of the master evoker intensifi es arcane evocation spells you cast. Many an ambitious evoker sees gaining this vest as a rite of passage, much like casting his fi rst magic missile or fi reball, and among elite warmages (CAr 10) it serves as an unparalleled status symbol. Three times per day, you can activate the vest to enhance the next arcane evocation spell you cast before the end of your turn. That spell deals additional damage equal to twice its spell level, and the save DC of the spell increases by 2. This damage is of the same type as normal for the spell (or, if the spell deals more than one type of damage, of any one of those types at your discretion). If the spell doesn’t normally deal damage, this use of the vest has no effect. In addition, you can apply the effect of any sudden metamagic feat (CAr 83) you know to any evocation spell you cast from a wand or staff as if you were casting the spell yourself. Doing this requires no activation, but uses up that feat’s daily use as normal. Vestments of ebonsilk Vest of defense Vest of resistance Vest of the archmagi

Fiery tunic.description: This eye-catching tunic shifts and moves like liquid fl ame. A crystal shard burns a brilliant red in the middle of the chest, fused seamlessly with the cloth of the shirt. A fi ery tunic grants you resistance to fi re 5. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. When you activate the tunic, it generates a fi re shield, as the spell (warm shield effect only), that lasts for 5 rounds. In addition, any melee weapons you hold while the fi re shield is active are treated as fl aming weapons (DMG 224). You can deactivate this effect at any time with Fiery tunic

Lightning tunic.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges creates an electrical field around your body for 3 rounds, dealing electricity damage to anyone striking you with a natural attack or nonreach melee weapon (does not harm you or your gear).

1 charge: 2d6.
2 charges: 3d6.
3 charges: 4d6.

Tunic of steady spellcasting.description: This earth-colored tunic is sturdy, though the material is as soft as silk. Around the neck, wrists, and hem are runes of stability stitched in gold thread. You gain a +5 competence bonus on Concentration checks. Despite the name, the tunic’s bonus applies on all Concentration checks, not just those made to cast spells.

Burnoose of 1,001 thorns.description: Thousands of strange pores cover this robe of thick green cloth, making it look like the parched skin of some desert plant. A burnoose of 1,001 thorns grants you a +1 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor bonus. (A creature without natural armor has an effective natural armor bonus of +0.) You also gain a +4 bonus on saves to resist becoming dehydrated from heat or exposure (but gain no protection from desiccation damage, such as a horrid wilting spell, or spells that cause you to become dehydrated). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. When activated, a burnoose sprouts hundreds of sharp thorns, which last for 5 rounds. Any creature that strikes you with a natural weapon or makes a grapple check against you (except to escape your grapple or pin) takes 1d6 points of piercing damage from the needles. This ability functions once per day.

Dispelling cord.description:
Worn wrapped loosely around your chest. When activated, you gain a +2 competence bonus on any dispel checks you make until the end of your turn.

Functions five times per day.

Impervious vestment.description: This black silk robe is embroidered with adamantine thread in an elegant waterfall pattern. An impervious vestment grants a +5 armor bonus to your Armor Class. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, you can command it once per day to create a blade barrier as a ringed wall of whirling blades only (20 feet high, up to 90 feet in diameter, centered on your current location). This effect lasts for 18 minutes or until dismissed as a standard action. As long as you wear the vestment, you can pass through your barrier without harm, though you receive no such protection against any other blade barrier.

Incarnum vestments.description:
Matches the Heart chakra (torso slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Rags of restraint.description: When activated, expend a daily use of Stunning Fist (monk) or ki power (ninja) to heal damage equal to your monk or ninja level (or the sum of both if you have both classes).

Shiftweave.description: This otherwise plain-looking set of clothing seems to have gossamer threads woven randomly into the fabric. When you activate shiftweave, it changes your garb to resemble any of fi ve specifi c Shadow veil outfi ts designated during its creation. Shiftweave has no effect on any armor you wear (nor can it mimic armor) and does not change the effect of any magical clothing you wear. Lore: Though originally created for wealthy socialites who wanted to avoid wearing the same gown to the gala, shiftweave is also useful to spies and assassins (Knowledge [nobility and royalty]

Surcoat Of Valor.description: This is a fi ne blue linen surcoat, with gold and white trim and a golden dragon rampant emblazoned on the chest. If you are a knight (PH2 24), a surcoat of valor grants you various benefi ts associated with your challenge ability. You can increase the save DC of your knight’s challenge by 1. If you also wear a magic item that provides a bonus on Will saves (or on all saves), the duration of your knight’s challenge increases by 1 round. Despite the described appearance, a surcoat automatically changes its colors and image to match your offi cial symbol or heraldry.

Tabard Of The Great CrusadeR.description: This silvery tabard is emblazoned with a stylized sunburst design. Because of its association with uncommon valor and amazing healing power, a tabard of the great crusade is a welcome sight at any scene of armed confl ict. When you wear one of these garments over armor, you and every ally within 20 feet gains a +1 morale bonus on saving throws, provided that you are lawful neutral, lawful good, lawful evil, or neutral. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. Relic Power: If you have established the proper divine connection, a tabard of the great crusade also provides you with damage reduction 5/evil. Furthermore, any ally other than you who grasps the hem of the tabard (a move [manipulation] action) can gain the benefi t of a heal spell. This ability functions once per day. To use the relic power, you must worship St. Cuthbert and either sacrifi ce an 8th-level divine spell slot or have the True Believer feat and at least 15 HD. Lore: Only seven of these tabards are known to exist. Originally made for an elite cadre of warrior-healers, they have since been passed down from one battlefi eld cleric to another (Knowledge [religion] DC 20).

Tabard of the disembodiedR.description: This misty gray tabard is silky soft to the touch. It is embroidered with small sequins in a skull motif. A tabard of the disembodied is connected to the Ethereal Plane, the realm where ghosts hold sway. Once per day, you can activate this tabard to become ethereal for 1 round, provided that you are neutral evil, lawful evil, chaotic evil, or neutral. Relic Power: If you have established the proper divine connection, you can activate a tabard of the disembodied to transport yourself to the Ethereal Plane (as the ethereal jaunt spell) for up to 10 rounds per day. This duration need not be continuous, and the effect is dismissable with another standard (mental) action. While you are on the Ethereal Plane, the spells you cast affect the Material Plane normally, unlike those of spellcasters who reach the Ethereal Plane by other means. To use the relic power, you must worship Nerull and either sacrifi ce a 7th-level divine spell slot or have the True Believer feat and at least 13 HD. Lore: The fi rst of these tabards is said to have been among the burial raiments of Shedlazzar IV, a legendary wizardking who haunted his castle for decades after his death. His grandson, a noted necromancer in his own right, wrested the secret of the tabard’s creation from his ancestor’s ghost using an exceptionally cruel torture technique designed to psychically fl ay the spirit (Knowledge [religion] DC 20).

Armbands of Might.description: While worn, you gain a +2 bonus on Strength checks and Strength-based skill checks. If you have the Power Attack feat, you also gain a +2 bonus on melee damage rolls for any attack on which you use Power Attack and take a penalty of at least -2 on your attack roll.

Bracers of Archery, Greater.description: +2 competence bonus on ranged attack rolls with bows.

Bracers of Archery, Lesser.description: +1 competence bonus on ranged attack rolls with bows.

Bracers of Armor +1.description: +1 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +2.description: +2 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +3.description: +3 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +4.description: +4 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +5.description: +5 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +6.description: +6 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +7.description: +7 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Bracers of Armor +8.description: +8 armor bonus to AC (does not stack with worn armor).

Armband of confrontationSP.description: Grants a +2 competence bonus on Intimidate checks and allows demoralizing as a move action 1/day.

Armband of elusive action.description:
When activated, allows you to avoid provoking a single attack of opportunity that your actions would otherwise incur.

Functions once per day.

Armband of maximized healing.description:
When activated, the next healing spell of 6th level or lower that you cast on your turn is automatically maximized (as the Maximize Spell feat, but with no adjustment to spell level or casting time).

Functions three times per day.

Bracers of accuracy.description:
The bracers have 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges grants a benefit to all ranged attacks you make before the end of your turn.

1 charge: Ignore AC bonus from cover (except total cover) for all targets.
2 charges: Ignore miss chance from concealment (except total concealment), including blur or displacement (but not incorporeality), for all targets.
3 charges: Ignore both cover and concealment for all targets.

Bracers of aerial combatSP.description: Grants a +2 enhancement bonus on attack rolls while flying 1/day.

Bracers of arcane freedom.description:
When activated, you omit the somatic component of the next arcane spell you cast before the end of your turn (as if applying the Still Spell feat, but without altering the spell's level or casting time).

Functions two times per day.

Bracers of blinding strikeSP.description: Allows blinding a creature with a strike 1/day.

Bracers of dawn.description:
Turning the dial to the middle position illuminates your surroundings as a bullseye lantern would (move action to adjust dial). Four times per day, you can turn the dial to the right position as a standard action and aim the lens at a target, unleashing a searing light effect (as the spell). The dial automatically returns to the middle position after each use.

Both bracers must be worn for the magic to be effective.

Bracers of great collision.description:
When you score a successful critical hit with a bludgeoning melee weapon, you can activate the bracers before rolling damage to deal an extra 2d6 points of damage with that attack (or 3d6 if you wield the weapon with two hands).

Functions two times per day.

Bracers of lightningSP.description: Deals 2d6 electricity damage to attackers who hit the wearer in melee 1/day.

Bracers of opportunity.description:
If you have the Combat Reflexes feat, you gain a +2 competence bonus on any attack of opportunity you make. This is a continuous effect.

When activated, you can take an attack of opportunity granted to you by an opponent, even if you have already reached your normal limit of attacks of opportunity in the round.

Functions two times per day.

Bracers of quick strike.description:
When activated, you can make one extra attack with any weapon you are holding if you already made a full attack on this turn. This attack is made at your full base attack bonus, plus any modifiers appropriate to the situation. Not cumulative with other effects that grant an extra attack on a full attack.

Functions once per day. You must wear the bracers for 24 hours before you can access their abilities; if you take them off, they become inactive until worn for another 24 hours.

Bracers of repulsion.description:
When activated, all enemies within 10 feet must succeed on a DC 19 Fortitude save or be pushed 5 feet away from you. Huge and larger creatures are unaffected, though incorporeal creatures can be affected.

Functions three times per day.

Bracers of retaliation.description: These hard leather armguards look sturdy, yet fl exible. Bracers of retaliation protect you from attacks and punish attackers for injuring you. They grant you a +1 armor bonus to AC (as bracers of armor). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, when you are dealt damage by a melee attack, you can activate the bracers and sacrifi ce an arcane spell or spell slot of 1st level or higher to daze your attacker for 1 round. A successful Will save (DC 10 + the level of the spell sacrifi ced) negates this effect. This effect functions three times per day.

Bracers of the blast barrier.description:
When activated, the next spell you cast or spell-like ability you use before the end of your turn instead appears as a 10-foot-long, 10-foot-high opaque wall that lasts for 1 round per level of the spell. One end of the wall must be placed at any corner of your space. If the wall's surface is broken by any object or creature, the wall fails to form and the spell and activation are lost.

Any creature passing through the wall takes damage equal to that normally dealt by the spell (minimum 1 point per spell level). A successful Reflex save (normal DC for a spell of that level cast by you) halves this damage.

Functions three times per day.

Bracers of the entangling blast.description:
When activated, the next spell you cast or spell-like ability you use deals only half its normal damage; however, any creature damaged by the spell becomes entangled for 1d3 rounds, taking an additional 1 point of damage per level of the spell each round on your turn (same damage type as the spell). No effect on a spell or spell-like ability that doesn't deal damage.

Functions three times per day.

Bracers of wind.description:
Upon activation, winds around you impose a -2 penalty on ranged attacks made against you (as if fired in a strong wind). Siege weapons and thrown boulders are unaffected. Does not stack if area wind is already strong or greater. While activated, you gain a +1 competence bonus on ranged attack rolls.

Say "calm" in Auran to suppress the winds (and benefits). Say "zephyr" while focusing on the etchings to reactivate.

Goodberry bracelet.description:
Each day at dawn while worn, creates five silver berry-shaped charms. When plucked, a charm becomes an edible berry with magical properties equivalent to goodberry, except no character can benefit from more than one berry per day. All charms and berries disappear 24 hours after creation.

If you also wear a magic item that grants a Wisdom bonus, add half that bonus to the healing each berry provides (e.g., periapt of Wisdom +4 creates berries that heal 3 points instead of 1).

Wand bracelet.description: This golden chain bracelet has a number of small metal objects hanging from it like charms. A wand bracelet can store up to four items, which appear as charms along the bracelet. You can grab one of the charms from the bracelet as a swift (manipulation) action, causing it to appear in your hand. If you have an item in hand, you can use a move (manipulation) action to store the item in the bracelet or to switch a held item for a stored item by touching the item to the charm representing the stored item. Storing an item causes it to shrink down and appear as a charm hanging from the bracelet. Any item stored can weigh no more than 3 pounds and must be able to be held in one hand, such as a wand or a light weapon. Only the wearer of the bracelet is able to retrieve or store items. Lore: The name wand bracelet describes the most common use of this item. Originally designed to hold small keepsakes as charms, and crafted to be appropriate for formal gatherings, wand bracelets have found popularity among artifi cers (ECS 29) who favor spell trigger devices (Knowledge [history] DC 15). These bracelets are also sometimes known as assassins’ charms, because small weapons can easily be concealed within them (Knowledge [history] DC 20).

Caduceus bracers.description:
By sacrificing 5 points of healing (from lay on hands, wholeness of body, or similar daily healing pool), you can remove 1 point of ability damage or remove the dazed, fatigued, or sickened condition from one creature. Extra points have cumulative effect. Follows all normal limitations of your healing ability (e.g., paladins must touch the target; monks can affect only themselves with wholeness of body).

You can combine normal healing with the bracers' effect.

Counterstrike bracers.description:
You can activate the bracers immediately after an enemy has missed you with a melee attack. Doing so grants you a free attack of opportunity against that enemy, even if you have already used your allotted attacks of opportunity for the round. You must activate before the enemy takes any other attacks or actions.

Functions two times per day.

Crimson dragonhide bracers.description: These leather bracers are crafted from the scaly red hide of a large reptile. Crimson dragonhide bracers grant you resistance to fi re 5 and a +1 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor bonus. (A creature without natural armor has an effective natural armor bonus of +0.)

Deathguardian bracers.description:
When activated, choose a prepared arcane spell or uncast spell slot. You lose that spell or slot and gain damage reduction equal to twice the level of the spell sacrificed. This DR lasts until the start of your next turn and cannot be overcome by any weapon type.

Can be activated as long as you have arcane spells remaining to sacrifice.

Deathstrike bracers.description:
When activated, you can use melee weapons to deal extra damage from critical hits and sneak attacks to constructs, elementals, oozes, plants, and undead as if they were not immune. You must still confirm a critical hit or qualify for sneak attack damage. Does not overcome other immunities (such as fortification). This effect lasts for 1 round.

Functions three times per day.

Greatreach bracers.description:
When activated, your arms stretch and elongate, extending your reach by 10 feet for 1 round.

Functions three times per day.

Inquisitor bracers.description:
When activated, the next melee attack you make before the end of your turn includes a cure moderate wounds effect (in addition to normal damage). Can't be used as part of a touch attack—only a normal melee attack.

Functions three times per day.

Rapidstrike bracers.description:
Increase the accuracy of a monk's flurry of blows. When activated, gain a +2 competence bonus on attack rolls made as part of a flurry of blows until the end of your turn.

Functions three times per day.

Reciprocal bracers.description:
If you score a hit in melee against someone who scored a critical hit or sneak attack against you since your last turn, activate to automatically consider your attack roll a critical threat (must still confirm as normal).

Functions two times per day.

Strongarm bracers.description: These fl exible, blue-green leather armbands are covered with gold studs. When wearing strongarm bracers, you can wield weapons as if you were one size category larger than normal. (You don’t take any penalty when using weapons as normal for your size.) For example, a human wearing these bracers could wield Large or Medium weapons without penalty. The effect of these bracers doesn’t stack with the powerful build trait (such as that possessed by the half-giant and goliath races).

Hexbands.description:
While worn, increase the save DC of your hexblade's curse by 1 (continuous, no activation).

When activated, add your Charisma bonus (if any) on weapon damage rolls against any creature currently affected by your hexblade's curse. Lasts 1 round.

Functions five times per day.

Incarnum bracers.description:
Matches the Arms chakra (arms slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Mithral bells.description: A number of tiny silvery bells adorn this glistening bracelet, each sculpted with swirling and curving embellishments. As long as at least one of the eleven bells that adorn this bracelet remain, it provides a +2 competence bonus on Perform checks. The bells are easily detached, allowing you to remove and throw one up to 40 feet. When it reaches the end of its trajectory, the bell explodes in a sound burst, as the spell. Once activated, a bell is expended and cannot be used again. When the last bell is removed, the bracelet loses all magic.

Shadow Maniple.description: This wristband seethes with ever-shifting shadows. Its touch feels like frozen silk. Actually a small rift between the Plane of Shadow and the Material Plane, a shadow maniple allows characters adept at shadow magic to derive healing from their use of such magic. When you cast a spell with the shadow descriptor, or whenever you cast a mystery (see the shadowcaster class, ToM 111), you can activate the maniple to heal damage equal to the level of the spell or mystery. A shadow maniple functions three times per day.

Vambraces Of Warding.description: These golden bracers are engraved with holy sigils. Vambraces of warding fi t snugly over the arms of any creature that dons them. A good-aligned wearer gains a +2 sacred bonus to AC against ranged attacks.

Spellmight bracers.description: When casting a spell that requires a touch attack and deals hit point damage, you can take a –5 penalty on the attack roll to deal an extra 1d6 points of damage with the spell. A duskblade can use these bracers when delivering a touch spell through a melee weapon, even if the spell doesn't deal hit point damage; the weapon (rather than the spell) deals an extra 1d6 points of damage.

Gloves of Swimming and Climbing.description: +5 competence bonus on Swim and Climb checks.

Glove of Storing.description: Stores one item up to 20 lb.

Casting glove.description: Functions as a glove of storing, allowing you to store or retrieve a single item as a free action. In addition, once per round while wearing the glove, you can activate or consume a stored magic item as if you were holding it in your hand.

Gauntlet of Gruumsh.description:
This +1 gauntlet allows you to make a melee touch attack to blind a target for 1 hour (Fort DC 14 negates). Elves take a -4 penalty on this save.

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlet of infinite blades.description:
When activated, a dagger appears in the gauntleted hand and disappears 3 rounds later. This functions as often as needed.

The gauntlet has 5 charges, renewed each day at dawn.
1 charge: +3 dagger.
3 charges: +3 dagger of seeking.
5 charges: +3 bane dagger of seeking.

Gauntlets of arrow reflection.description:
When you use the Deflect Arrows feat to deflect a ranged weapon attack, you can activate the gauntlets to cause the weapon to fly back at the attacker using the attacker's original attack roll and normal damage.

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of destructionSP.description: Grants a +2 enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls 1/day.

Gauntlets of eldritch energy.description:
When activated, the next eldritch blast you use before the end of your turn deals energy damage instead of its normal damage type (cold, electricity, fire, or sonic, depending on the variety of gauntlets). Cannot apply to a blast already affected by an eldritch essence invocation.

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of energy transformation.description:
When activated, change one type of energy damage produced by a melee weapon you hold into another type (acid, cold, electricity, or fire) for 1 round. If a weapon produces multiple energy types, each activation affects only one type. No effect if the weapon deals no energy damage.

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of extended range.description: Each of these well-made gauntlets is set with small purple crystals that rest across the tops of the knuckles. Whenever you throw a weapon or other object, gauntlets of extended range give the weapon a telekinetic push, doubling its range increment.

Gauntlets of giantfelling.description:
When activated, gain bonus damage on the next weapon damage roll against a creature larger than you before the end of your turn: +1d6 (1 size category larger), +2d6 (2 categories), +3d6 (3 or more categories).

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of lassitude.description: Three times per day, you can make a melee touch attack with these gauntlets. On a hit, the target is slowed for 5 rounds (Will DC 14 negates).

Gauntlets of mindfire.description: Once per day, projects a 15-foot cone of mental sickness (Fort DC 14 negates). A creature that fails contracts mindfire: immediate 1d4 Intelligence damage, then 1d4 Intelligence damage each day on failed DC 14 Fortitude saves until two consecutive successful saves. A creature can be infected only once.

Gauntlets of the blazing arc.description:
When activated, creates a 15-foot cone-shaped burst of flame dealing 4d4+4 points of fire damage (Reflex DC 14 half).

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of the blood-lordR.description:
Function as +1 spiked gauntlets if you are lawful evil, lawful neutral, or neutral evil.

Relic power (worship Hextor and sacrifice a 4th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 7 HD): +2 luck bonus on melee touch attacks. If you sacrifice a 6th-level divine spell or have True Believer and 11 HD, every 3rd-level or lower melee touch spell you cast is automatically empowered (no higher slot or increased casting time).

Gauntlets of the talonR.description:
If you are lawful good, lawful neutral, or neutral good, grants two primary natural claw attacks (1d6 slashing each, Medium) that count as magic weapons.

Relic power (worship Bahamut and sacrifice a 3rd-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 5 HD): if you have monk levels, treated as five monk levels higher for AC, unarmed damage, and ki strike; unarmed damage may be bludgeoning or slashing. With a 5th-level divine sacrifice or True Believer and 9 HD, claws also function as ghost touch and you gain +4 on grapple checks.

Gauntlets of throwing.description:
When activated, grant any one melee weapon you hold the throwing and returning properties for 1 round.

Functions three times per day.

Gauntlets of weaponry arcane.description: Any weapon (even nonmetallic) you hold while wearing these gauntlets overcomes damage reduction as if it were a magic silvered weapon.

Gloves of agile striking.description:
When activated, deal an extra 1d6 damage with ranged weapons and light weapons (and nonlight Weapon Finesse weapons). If delivering a skirmish attack, the bonus improves to 2d6. Lasts 1 round.

Functions two times per day.

Gloves of eldritch admixture.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges adds acid, cold, or fire damage to the next eldritch blast before the end of your turn.

1 charge: +2d6.
2 charges: +3d6.
3 charges: +4d6.

Gloves of flame.description:
Continuous endure elements against cold and resistance to cold 5.

Five times per day, utter "burn" in Ignan to create torch-bright flame in one palm for up to 10 minutes. Command words then extinguish it:
"Scorch": 15-foot cone, 5d4 fire (Reflex DC 11 half).
"Singe": melee touch attack for 1d6+5 fire damage.

Gloves of fortunate striking.description:
When you activate these gloves immediately after missing with a melee or ranged attack, you can reroll that attack roll. You must use the second result.

Functions three times per day.

Gloves of incarnum theft.description:
Three times per day, make a melee touch attack against a creature that has essentia. If successful, the target loses 1 point of essentia (Will DC 16 negates). The stolen essentia can immediately be invested in any soulmeld, incarnum feat, or other essentia receptacle with no action required, even if essentia investment wouldn't normally be allowed at this time. If you have no means of using essentia, you gain no benefit, but the target still loses the point. The stolen essentia remains in your essentia pool for 12 minutes, then returns to the original possessor.

Chakra bind (Hands): If bound to your hands chakra, you instead steal 2 points of essentia with a successful touch attack, and the save DC increases by 2.

Gloves of lightning.description: Three times per day, make a ranged touch attack (range 30 feet) that deals 1d8+5 electricity damage. You gain a +3 bonus on the attack if the target is wearing metal armor (or is made of metal, is carrying a lot of metal, and so on).

Gloves of manual prowess.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges grants a competence bonus on the next Disable Device, Forgery, Open Lock, Sleight of Hand, or Use Rope check you make (must begin within 1 round of activation; can't apply when taking 10 or 20).

1 charge: +5.
2 charges: +7.
3 charges: +10.

Gloves of object reading.description:
While wearing these gloves and handling an inanimate object, learn details about its previous owner through psychic impressions. Study time required:
1st minute: last owner's race.
2nd minute: gender.
3rd minute: age.
4th minute: alignment.
5th minute: how last owner gained and lost the object.
6th+ minute: next-to-last owner's race, and so on.

Does not identify casual users as owners. Objects without owners, or without owners in the past ten years, reveal no information. Repeated use on the same object yields the same information.

Gloves of spell disruption.description: If you hit a creature with a melee attack while it is casting a spell, add 5 to the DC of the Concentration check required to avoid losing the spell.

Gloves of the balanced hand.description: While worn, you fight as though you possess Two-Weapon Fighting. If you already have Two-Weapon Fighting, you can make an additional off-hand attack as if using Improved Two-Weapon Fighting.

Gloves of the starry skySP.description: Grants a +2 competence bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks.

Gloves of the titan’s grip.description:
While wearing these gloves, gain a +8 enhancement bonus on grapple checks. Automatically activates when you enter a grapple; lasts 7 rounds.

Functions three times per day.

Gloves of the uldra savant.description:
While wearing these gloves, create a ray of frost at will.

Three times per day, imbue any melee weapon held in your hand with the frost property for 5 rounds.

Mind blade gauntlet.description:
Created with a particular weapon property with a +2 market price modifier (such as collision) usable by a soulknife with the mind blade enhancement class feature. When you expend psionic focus while manifesting your mind blade, the blade gains that property for 1 minute.

If you split your mind blade into two, the effect applies only to the blade manifested through this gauntlet. You can wear two mind blade gauntlets simultaneously (one effect per blade, not two on one blade).

Mind blade gauntlet, greater.description: Functions as a mind blade gauntlet, except it holds a weapon property with a +3 market price modifier usable by a soulknife with the mind blade enhancement class feature.

Mind blade gauntlet, lesser.description: Functions as a mind blade gauntlet, except it holds a weapon property with a +1 market price modifier usable by a soulknife with the mind blade enhancement class feature.

Adamantine mind blade gauntlets.description:
If you can enhance your mind blade, whenever you materialize it while wearing these gauntlets you can choose to treat it as an adamantine weapon for ignoring hardness and overcoming damage reduction. This ability takes the place of a property with a +1 enhancement bonus value.

Functions an unlimited number of times per day; using it is part of the action to materialize your mind blade.

Arcanist’s gloves.description:
When activated, add 2 to the caster level of the next 1st-level arcane spell you cast before the end of your turn.

Functions two times per day.

Brawler’s gauntlets.description:
When activated, for 1 round, grants a +2 bonus on grapple checks and damage rolls when making unarmed attacks.

Functions three times per day.

Brute gauntlets.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges grants a morale bonus on Strength checks, Strength-based skill checks, and melee weapon damage for 1 round.

1 charge: +2 morale bonus.
2 charges: +3 morale bonus.
3 charges: +4 morale bonus.

Devastation gauntlets.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. After confirming a critical hit in melee (but before rolling damage), spending charges adds bonus damage.

1 charge: +2d6 points of damage.
2 charges: +3d6 points of damage.
3 charges: +4d6 points of damage.

Dragonfang gauntlets.description: Function as masterwork spiked gauntlets and grant a +2 enhancement bonus to Strength. Three times per day, you can activate them to treat your next melee attack against a weapon or shield as though you had Improved Sunder. If you have Improved Unarmed Strike, your unarmed strikes overcome damage reduction as magic weapons and deal unarmed strike damage instead of spiked gauntlet damage.

Finned gauntlets.description: Sweeping fins adorn these sharkskin gauntlets. You gain a natural swim speed of 30 feet (which also grants you a +8 bonus on Swim checks to perform some special action or avoid a hazard). You can always take 10 on a Swim check, even if distracted or endangered, and can use the run action while swimming.

Ghoul gauntlets.description: No effect on constructs or undead. Living wearers can once per round make a melee touch attack that paralyzes the target for 1d6+2 rounds (Fort DC 13 negates; elves are immune). Can also be delivered through an unarmed strike, slam, claw, or similar hand natural attack (normal attack, not touch).

Hellcat gauntlets.description:
When activated, the next spell you cast during your turn that targets a single creature also deals 1d6 slashing damage per level of the spell, unless the spell has no effect on the target (spell resistance or a save negating the spell, for example), in which case the extra damage is negated.

Functions three times per day, but can't be activated in consecutive rounds.

Lightning gauntlets.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges deals electricity damage to an adjacent target (no attack roll; Reflex DC 14 halves).

1 charge: 2d6.
2 charges: 3d6.
3 charges: 4d6.

Mesmerist’s gloves.description:
When you cast an enchantment spell of 3rd level or lower that targets an enemy, activate to have the spell also target another enemy within 10 feet of the original target.

Functions two times per day.

Reach gauntlets.description:
If you are a psionic character, spend 1 power point to activate, extending your normal reach by 5 feet for all melee attacks you make until the end of your turn.

Functions three times per day.

Rending gauntlets.description: These heavy, fi ngerless leather gloves bear shining adamantine bands across the knuckles. These masterwork spiked gauntlets allow you to tear the fl esh of any creature you strike with at least two weapons. If you deal damage to a creature with at least two different weapons or natural attacks on your turn, you can activate rending gauntlets to deal an extra 2d6 points of damage with the second attack. This ability functions three times per day.

Repelling gauntlets.description:
Activate only when a visible enemy moves into a square adjacent to you. That enemy must attempt a DC 17 Reflex save; on failure, it is immediately pushed 5 feet away and loses any remaining movement allowed in that action (but can use additional actions to move farther).

Functions once per day.

Soulsmite gauntlets.description: Each of these white steel gauntlets has a smooth cerulean gem mounted on the wrist. Soulsmite gauntlets allow a soulborn (MoI 25) to channel the power of a soulmeld into her smite opposition ability. When you use smite opposition, you can select one soulmeld you have shaped. All essentia invested in that soulmeld becomes uninvested, and you gain an additional bonus on your attack roll and damage roll equal to the essentia that was invested. For example, if you were an 8thlevel soulborn with 2 essentia invested in your impulse boots, you could drain that soulmeld temporarily to gain an additional +2 bonus on the attack roll and damage roll of your smite attack. Essentia drained from a soulmeld in this way returns to your essentia pool and can be invested again as normal. Chakra Bind (Hands): If you bind soulsmite gauntlets to your hands chakra, you gain one additional daily use of your smite opposition ability. When the gauntlets are bound, the gems mounted on them glow with soft inner light. See Magic of Incarnum for rules on binding magic items to your chakras.

Storm gauntlets.description: Ribbons of electricity crackle over these gleaming, blue-tinged metal gauntlets. Jagged white bolts arc between the pair, fi lling the air with the scent of ozone. When worn together, this pair of +1 spiked gauntlets grants you resistance to electricity 5. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, storm gauntlets have 4 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges allows you to generate a number of electrcity-based effects. 1 charge: shocking grasp. 2 charges: lightning bolt (Refl ex DC 14 half). 4 charges: electric spheres. These act like fl aming spheres, except that you create four spheres of crackling electricity. You can direct any or all of the spheres with the same action. Multiple spheres directed at the same creature deal damage separately (Refl ex DC 13 half). These spheres deal electric damage.

Toxic gloves.description: These smooth black leather gloves feel slightly sticky to the touch. Toxic gloves allow you to imbue a held weapon with poison without any risk to yourself. When you activate these gloves, choose a single melee weapon you hold. The next attack made with that weapon before the end of your turn also poisons the target (injury; 1d6 Con/1d6 Con; Fort DC 16 negates). This ability functions three times per day.

True strike gauntlets.description: These supple leather gauntlets are dyed the color of ivory. When you activate true strike gauntlets, you gain a +20 insight bonus on the next attack you make before the end of your next turn. This ability functions once per day.

Ki straps.description: When wrapped around both hands, grant a +2 enhancement bonus to the DC of your Stunning Fist attack (or any other effect gained by expending daily uses of that feat).

Incarnum gauntlets.description:
Matches the Hands chakra (wrists slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Ring of Animal Friendship.description: On command, charm animal as charm animal spell.

Ring of Chameleon Power.description: +10 competence bonus on Hide checks; disguise self at will.

Ring of Counterspells.description: Stores one spell (1st-6th) to automatically counterspell when cast on wearer.

Ring of Djinni Calling.description: Calls a specific djinni for up to 1 hour/day.

Ring of Elemental Command (Air).description: +2 resistance bonus on saves vs. air-elemental attacks; +4 morale on attacks vs. them.

Ring of Elemental Command (Earth).description: +2 resistance bonus on saves vs. earth-elemental attacks; +4 morale on attacks vs. them.

Ring of Elemental Command (Fire).description: +2 resistance bonus on saves vs. fire-elemental attacks; +4 morale on attacks vs. them.

Ring of Elemental Command (Water).description: +2 resistance bonus on saves vs. water-elemental attacks; +4 morale on attacks vs. them.

Ring of Evasion.description: Evasion as if wearer had the evasion ability.

Ring of Feather Falling.description: Feather fall when wearer falls more than 5 ft.

Ring of Force Shield.description: +2 AC heavy shield (wall of force); no armor check penalty or arcane spell failure.

Ring of Friend Shield (pair).description: Shield other to mated ring at will.

Ring of Mind Shielding.description: Immune to detect thoughts, discern lies, and magical alignment detection.

Ring of Regeneration.description: Heal 1 HP/level per hour; regenerate limbs.

Ring of Shooting Stars.description: Dancing lights, light, ball lightning, shooting stars.

Ring of Spell Turning.description: Reflect up to 9 spell levels per day.

Ring of Sustenance.description: Life-sustaining nourishment; wearer needs only 2 hours of sleep per day.

Ring of Three Wishes.description: Three wish spells; rubies consumed on use.

Ring of X-Ray Vision.description: See through solid matter on command (20 ft.).

Bone ring.description: While worn, you are protected from energy drain and ability drain (but not ability damage). The ring has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Every negative level or point of ability drain prevented uses 1 charge.

Brute ring.description:
When activated, a blast of force makes a bull rush attack against a single creature up to 30 feet from you. Use either the ring's check modifier (+5) or your own bull rush check modifier, whichever is higher. As a force effect, this affects incorporeal creatures.

Functions two times per day.

Life ring.description: Grants a +2 resistance bonus on saves against death effects.

Lockpicking ring.description: This large ring is set with a faceted ruby. Closer inspection reveals a tiny trigger near the base of the stone. Hidden below the stone in this ring is a set of tiny prongs, wires, and other small devices that spring out when the trigger is depressed. Using a lockpicking ring grants you a +5 competence bonus on Open Lock checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, you can activate the ring once per day to use knock. You must touch the ring to the portal you want to open.

Ring of adamantine touch.description: While worn, your melee attacks (natural or with a weapon) are treated as adamantine for overcoming damage reduction.

Ring of antivenom.description: A tiny, speckled mushroom grows from the tip of this granite ring. Once per day, you can activate the ring to gain the benefi t of a neutralize poison effect.

Ring of arcane might.description: While worn, gain a +1 bonus to your arcane caster level for spell penetration checks, caster level checks, and all level-based variables of any arcane spells you cast.

Ring of arming.description: When activated, any armor or weapons you currently wear disappear and are stored magically within the ring, and any armor or weapons stored in the ring appear in the appropriate places on your body (held items appear at your feet if you lack free hands).

Ring of avoidance.description: Three times per day, upon activating, gain a +20 deflection bonus to AC until the beginning of your next turn.

Ring of brief blessing.description:
Good-aligned only. When activated, choose a single melee weapon you hold (or unarmed strike). Until end of turn, that weapon is magic and good-aligned for overcoming evil damage reduction and affecting incorporeal evil creatures.

Functions once per day; glow fades until recharged. While wearing the ring, expend a turn undead attempt as a standard action to recharge it (restores glow).

Ring of communication.description:
When activated, for 1 minute clearly hear any words spoken by anyone within 1 mile who wears another attuned ring of communication (whispers audible above combat noise).

Touch rings together and speak the command word in unison (standard action) to attune. Any number of rings can attune together if all are touched at once. A ring stays attuned until attuned to a different set.

Ring of divergence.description:
Provides resistance 10 against one energy type (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic), determined at creation.

Once per day, if targeted by a spell of 3rd level or lower with the same descriptor as the ring's energy type, activate as an immediate action to reflect the spell back at the caster (as spell turning).

Ring of dragon friendship.description:
While worn, gain a +5 enhancement bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence creatures of the dragon type.

Once per day, activate to produce suggestion directed at a dragon (9th-level spell, Will DC 23). If you or an ally attacks a dragon while wearing the ring, it loses its powers for 24 hours (active suggestion continues while inactive).

Ring of entropic deflection.description: At the end of any turn, if you are at least 10 feet from where you started, creates a field functioning as entropic shield until the start of your next turn. Ranged attacks requiring an attack roll have a 20% miss chance (50% if you also wear an item that grants a speed bonus). Continuous, no activation.

Ring of essentia.description:
Any creature with an essentia pool can transfer 1 point into the ring by touch and will (standard action), reducing the donor's pool by 1. The ring holds no more than 1 point.

Within 24 hours, the wearer can use stored essentia as part of their own pool—even without essentia—by willing release while wearing the ring. Returns to the original owner after use, or after 24 hours if unused. The donor can reclaim it earlier by touch and will (standard action).

Ring of floating.description: A scene of a shattered ship and wooden debris fl oating on the waves covers this thick driftwood band. You fl oat upon any liquid or similar surface, and cannot swim below the surface. If you are underwater when you put on a ring of fl oating, you rise toward the surface at a speed of 30 feet per round.

Ring of four winds.description:
When activated, creates gusts of wind granting a +2 deflection bonus to AC against a single attack. Alternatively, activate to use feather fall on yourself.

Functions four times per day total.

Ring of greater counterspells.description:
Can hold a spell of 1st through 6th level (like ring of counterspells). You cannot cast the stored spell, but if that spell is cast upon you it is immediately countered with no action or knowledge required (uses up the stored spell).

Once per day as an immediate action, attempt to counter a single spell cast by a spellcaster you can see within 100 feet (no Spellcraft check; counterspell spell can be any level). Functions like greater dispel magic used to counter, adding your caster level (maximum +20).

Ring of improved climbing.description: Grants a +10 competence bonus on Climb checks 1/day.

Ring of improved jumping.description: Grants a +10 competence bonus on Jump checks 1/day.

Ring of improved swimming.description: Grants a +10 competence bonus on Swim checks 1/day.

Ring of lightning flashes.description:
When activated, emits a blast of lightning dealing 5d6 electricity damage (Reflex DC 13 half) to all creatures within 5 feet except you. Creatures in physical contact with you (grappling, engulfing, swallowing, etc.) receive no saving throw.

Functions three times per day.

Ring of master artifice.description: Functions only if you can imbue infusions. Assembling requires 5 minutes and DC 22 Intelligence check (retry allowed). Must wear 24 hours before magic takes effect. Increases infusions regained daily: one bonus 3rd-level infusion and one bonus 4th-level infusion each time you concentrate to regain your daily allotment.

Ring of master artifice, greater.description: Functions as a ring of master artifice, except it provides one bonus 5th-level infusion and one bonus 6th-level infusion (instead of 3rd and 4th).

Ring of master artifice, lesser.description: Functions as a ring of master artifice, except it provides one bonus 1st-level infusion and one bonus 2nd-level infusion (instead of 3rd and 4th).

Ring of mystic defiance.description:
When activated, ignore up to 10 points of damage from a spell or spell-like ability (activate after learning damage but before it is dealt). Does not protect from non-spell magical effects unless they mimic spells. Functions three times per day.

If you also wear an item granting an Intelligence or Charisma enhancement bonus, add half that bonus as an insight bonus on Fortitude saves against spells and spell-like abilities (maximum +3). Continuous, no activation.

Ring of mystic fire.description:
When casting fire spells, gain a +1 competence bonus to caster level (continuous).

Has 3 charges, renewed at dawn. Spending charges adds bonus fire damage to the next fire spell you cast before end of turn (no effect if the spell doesn't deal fire damage):

1 charge: +2d6.
2 charges: +3d6.
3 charges: +4d6.

Ring of mystic healing.description:
When casting cure spells, gain a +1 competence bonus to caster level (continuous).

Has 3 charges, renewed at dawn. Spending charges adds bonus healing to the next cure spell you cast before end of turn:

1 charge: +2d6.
2 charges: +3d6.
3 charges: +4d6.

Ring of mystic lightning.description:
When casting electricity spells, gain a +1 competence bonus to caster level (continuous).

Has 3 charges, renewed at dawn. Spending charges adds bonus electricity damage to the next electricity spell you cast before end of turn (no effect if it doesn't deal electricity damage):

1 charge: +2d6.
2 charges: +3d6.
3 charges: +4d6.

Ring of negative protection.description: Ignore the 1d6 damage dealt by negative-dominant planes (such as the Negative Energy Plane) each round. Cannot gain negative levels.

Ring of nine lives.description:
If you drop to 0 hit points or below, automatically expends one charge to heal 20 points of damage (no action required).

Once per day as an immediate action, expend a charge when you fail a saving throw to treat it as a success.

Created with 9 charges; most found with only 2d4 charges remaining.

Ring of piercing spells.description:
When activated, the next melee touch spell you cast against an enemy you are flanking deals an extra 2d6 damage. Touch spells that don't deal hit point damage gain no benefit. No benefit if you don't cast such a spell before end of turn.

Functions three times per day.

Ring of positive protection.description: Ignore the blinding effect of positive-dominant planes (such as the Positive Energy Plane). Do not gain fast healing from such planes, nor extra hit points when at your normal maximum. Cure spells and other positive-energy channeling spells have no effect on you.

Ring of self-sufficiency.description: This fragile, crystal ring is pale green and etched in a swirling pattern that appears to move constantly.

Ring of silent spells.description:
When activated, creates a silence effect centered on you. While it lasts, cast up to three spells of 3rd level or lower without verbal components (as Silent Spell, but no higher slot or increased casting time).

Functions once per day.

Ring of soulbound protection.description: Gain a +1 deflection bonus to AC. The ring serves as an essentia receptacle; each point invested increases deflection bonus by 1 (maximum +3). Maximum essentia capacity 2 (or your normal essentia capacity, whichever is lower).

Ring of soulbound protection, greater.description: Functions as a ring of soulbound protection, except maximum AC bonus is +5 instead of +3. Maximum essentia capacity 4.

Ring of spell-battle.description:
Informs you of all spellcasting within 60 feet and allows Spellcraft (DC 15 + spell level) to identify spells cast (even if you can't see casting or effect). Continuous.

Once per day, when you successfully identify a spell, activate to counterspell it as dispel magic or change its target to any legal target within 60 feet of you.

Ring of the darkhidden.description: More than a dozen etchings of closed eyes circle this tarnished silver ring. You are invisible to darkvision, although you are fully visible in normal light.

Ring of the forcewall.description:
Has 3 charges, renewed each day at dawn. Spending charges creates a 10-foot-square vertical wall of force (as the spell) up to 30 feet away. Duration depends on charges spent:

1 charge: 2 rounds.
2 charges: 3 rounds.
3 charges: 4 rounds.

Ring of the icy soul.description: Gain resistance to cold 20, but also vulnerability to fire and lose any resistance or immunity to fire you already have. Take 1-1/2 times normal fire damage regardless of whether a saving throw is allowed or succeeds.

Ring of the ram.description: Deals 1d6 damage and can bull rush a creature 1/day.

Ring of thunderclaps.description:
Activate as a swift action to make a melee touch attack before end of turn dealing 1d8+5 electricity damage. Functions three times per day.

Once per day, after successfully damaging a target with that touch attack, activate as a free action to create a thunderclap mimicking shout.

Ring of universal energy resistance, gre.description: Functions as a ring of energy resistance for all energy types (fire, cold, electricity, acid, and sonic), granting resistance 30.

Ring of universal energy resistance, maj.description: Functions as a ring of energy resistance for all energy types (fire, cold, electricity, acid, and sonic), granting resistance 20.

Ring of universal energy resistance, min.description: Functions as a ring of energy resistance for all energy types (fire, cold, electricity, acid, and sonic), granting resistance 10.

Ring of vanishing.description:
When activated, become invisible (as greater invisibility) and undetectable by hearing, scent, blindsense, blindsight, or tremorsense for 2 rounds. Spells that detect invisible creatures (see invisibility, true seeing) still work normally.

Functions three times per day.

Ring of vengeance.description: Seven small half-spheres rise from the shining, refl ective surface of this golden ring. A ring of vengeance glows softly when worn by a good-aligned creature. Nongood creatures gain no benefi t from the ring. Whenever a creature scores a critical hit on you with a melee weapon, this ring unleashes an arc of divine energy that deals 5d6 points of damage to the creature that scored the critical hit. This ability functions three times per day. Lore: Originally given by archons to their mortal champions in the lost ages of the world, the oldest of these rings were ancient when the elves fi rst began to record their histories.

Ring of water breathing.description: This silver ring is shaped to depict a fi sh biting its own tail. The fi sh has tiny emeralds for eyes. While wearing this ring, you can breathe freely underwater. This has no effect on your ability to breathe air.

Rings of force armor (pair).description: Grants a +4 armor bonus to AC 1/day.

Belt of Dwarvenkind.description: +2 enhancement bonus to Constitution; +2 resistance bonus on saves vs. poison, spells, and spell-like effects; 60 ft. darkvision for non-dwarves.

Belt, Monk's.description: Unarmed AC and damage as monk 5 levels higher; +1 stunning fist/day if applicable.

Belt of battle.description:
A belt of battle helps you avoid being caught off guard in combat and allows occasional bursts of extra activity. While worn, it grants you a +2 competence bonus on initiative checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation.

In addition, a belt of battle has 3 charges/day, which are renewed at dawn. Each time you activate the belt, one of the black pearls set into its buckle turns white. The pearls return to normal when the belt's charges renew. Spending 1 or more charges grants you an extra action, which must be taken immediately (before you take any other action).

1 charge: 1 move action.
2 charges: 1 standard action.
3 charges: 1 full-round action.

Belt of growth.description:
When activated, you instantly increase in size (as enlarge person). This effect lasts for 10 minutes or until you command it to end. This ability does not stack with any other effect that increases your size.

Functions once per day.

Belt of hidden pouches.description:
Ten visible pockets and twenty hidden pockets (two behind each visible one), each functioning as a small bag of holding (1/2 cubic foot or 5 pounds; no object dimension over 6 inches). Contents weigh one-tenth normal weight. Hidden pockets require a command word to access.

Placing or retrieving an item is a move action (hidden-pocket placement does not provoke attacks of opportunity).

Belt of one mighty blow.description:
When activated, your next melee attack made before the end of your turn deals extra damage: light weapons +1d8, one-handed weapons +2d6, two-handed weapons +3d6.

Functions once per day.

Belt of priestly might.description:
Features a deity's holy symbol. Followers of that deity or anyone of that deity's alignment can wear it safely; any other creature gains one negative level while wearing it.

While worn, you gain a +1 enhancement bonus to your existing natural armor bonus and a +2 enhancement bonus to Strength.

Belt of the championR.description:
Functions as a belt of giant strength +2 if you are chaotic good, neutral good, or chaotic neutral.

Relic power (worship Kord and sacrifice a 3rd-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 5 HD): +5 competence bonus on Strength checks and +4 morale bonus on saves against fear. If you fail a save against fear while wearing this belt, its relic power is negated for 1 hour.

Belt of the wide earth.description:
While wearing a Belt of the Wide Earth your carrying capacity is doubled.

This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, 2/day you can activate the belt and sacrifice a prepared spell or spell slot of 5th level or higher to use teleport (as the spell), using your own caster level or that of the belt, whichever is higher. 

Grants: Teleport

Belt of theft-healing.description:
When you steal a spell or spell-like ability from an unwilling target, you can activate the belt to also heal damage equal to the level of the spell or spell-like ability stolen.

Functions three times per day.

Belt of ultimate athleticism.description:
When activated, you can take 10 on all Balance, Climb, Jump, Swim, and Tumble checks made in that round, even in a stressful situation.

In addition, once per day you can activate the belt to treat the next Balance, Climb, Jump, Swim, or Tumble check you attempt as if you had rolled a 20.

Gwaeron’s belt.description:
Two abilities, each once per day:
First command: your weapon bursts into flames (as the flaming weapon property) for 12 rounds.
Second command: wind walk effect affecting only you.

Healing belt.description: This broad leather belt is studded with three moonstones. While wearing a healing belt, you gain a +2 competence bonus on Heal checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, the belt has 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges allows you to channel positive energy and heal damage with a touch. (You can also use this ability to harm undead, dealing them an equivalent amount of damage instead.) 1 charge: Heals 2d8 points of damage. 2 charges: Heals 3d8 points of damage. 3 charges: Heals 4d8 points of damage.

Monk’s belt.description: Grants a +2 enhancement bonus to Wisdom and allows using monk abilities 1/day.

Silkslick belt.description: This belt is crafted from a thousand strands of silk, which are dyed the color of midnight. A silkslick belt is a boon to burglars and contortionists, but also to anyone who wants to avoid being caught in a tight spot. You can attempt an Escape Artist check to escape from a grapple or pin as a move action (rather than as a standard action). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, once per day, you can activate a silkslick belt to gain a +10 competence bonus on the next Escape Artist check you begin before the end of your next turn.

Desperation chain.description:
When you are being rendered helpless by an enemy, you can activate the chain to cast a single arcane spell of 3rd level or lower without spending an additional action. The spell requires no verbal or somatic components and does not provoke attacks of opportunity, but still requires material components, foci, XP, and other costs. Casting time must be no longer than 1 full round. The spell takes effect after the effect that rendered you helpless.

Functions once per day. You must wear the chain for 24 hours before you can access its abilities.

Dragon spirit cincture.description: While worn, your breath weapon damage is increased by one die (or by 1 point if your breath weapon does not deal damage expressed in dice). If you are also holding a magic weapon that deals the same type of damage as your breath weapon, your breath weapon save DC increases by 1.

Incarnum belt.description:
Matches the Waist chakra (waist slot). While worn, adds 1 to the essentia capacity of any soulmeld occupying or bound to the chakra this focus matches (up to your Constitution bonus). Wearing it does not prevent binding a soulmeld to that chakra.

Chakra bind (any): While bound to its chakra, soulmelds on that chakra can't be unshaped except by you, and essentia invested can't be lost or drained except during normal reallocation.

Spare Hand.description: This strange item consists of an animated arm and three-fi ngered claw attached to a heavy leather belt reinforced with strips of metal. As its most basic function, a spare hand can grasp or carry any object you could normally carry in one hand, freeing your normal hands for other tasks. A spare hand can hold magic items such as wands and rods for easy access, but you cannot activate those items while they are so held. Transferring an item to or from a spare hand is a free action. Whenever your spare hand is not carrying an item, you gain a +2 competence bonus on Climb, Escape Artist, and grapple checks. Without further augmentation from an infusion (see below), a spare hand cannot do anything other than hold an item—it cannot wield a weapon, retrieve a stored item, or perform any other task. If you power a spare hand with a 1st-level infusion (see the EBERRON Campaign Setting), it gains additional qualities. Once per round, you can command the hand to stow an item or retrieve a stowed item (including a weapon) as a free action. If you instead expend a 2nd-level infusion, the spare hand gains the above ability and can also hold a buckler or light shield, freeing your normal hand while still providing a bonus to AC from the shield. You still incur any armor check penalty or other drawbacks of holding the shield, and are subject to normal penalties if you are not profi cient with the shield. If you expend a 3rd-level infusion, a spare hand gains all the above abilities and becomes capable of wielding a light weapon. You can command a spare hand to make off-hand attacks with this weapon as if you were wielding it. A spare hand attacks using your normal attack modifi er plus any appropriate penalties for an off-hand attack. Using an infusion to power a spare hand requires 10 minutes and the expenditure of an infusion of the appropriate level. An infusion powers a spare hand for 24 hours.

Ruby cincture of immutability.description: This sumptuous red silk sash is feather-light. It is set with a large ruby cabochon surrounded by an embroidered silver circle. Wearing a ruby cincture of immutability provides you with a safeguard against attacks that change your form. Once per day, you can instantly counter any effect that would signifi cantly change your bodily form or composition. Effects that the sash protects against include polymorph, petrifi cation, disintegration, and transformative effects such as the touch of an aboleth’s tentacle or a chaos beast’s attack.

Boots of Elvenkind.description: +5 competence bonus on Move Silently checks in natural surroundings.

Boots of Speed.description: +30 ft. enhancement bonus to land speed; haste 10 rounds/day.

Boots of Striding and Springing.description: 5th-level long jump and high jump always; +5 competence on Jump checks.

Boots of the Winterlands.description: Warmth and endure elements in cold.

Horseshoes of a Zephyr.description: Mount walks above ground without tracks.

Boots of agile leaping.description:
While worn, you add your Dexterity modifier (instead of your Strength modifier) on Jump checks.

If you have at least 5 ranks in Balance, you can stand from prone as a swift action without provoking attacks of opportunity.

Boots of big stepping.description:
While worn, your caster level for all teleportation spells is increased by 2.

In addition, when you speak the command word, you can teleport up to 60 feet with no chance of error (as greater teleport). Functions three times per day.

Boots of desperation.description: These soft-soled boots look comfortable and quiet. You can activate boots of desperation only when your current hit point total is equal to or less than half your full normal hit points. When activated, the boots grant a +30-foot enhancement bonus to your base speed and a +5 dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity. These benefi ts last for 1 round. This ability functions three times per day.

Boots of dragonstriding.description:
While worn, you gain a +2 competence bonus on Climb and Jump checks.

Once per day, you can activate the boots to gain a climb speed equal to your land speed for 10 rounds.

Boots of jumping.description: These comfortable, black leather boots have unusually springy soles. Boots of jumping allow you to jump exceptionally long distances. You only need to move at least 10 feet in a straight line to attempt a running jump (rather than 20 feet). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, boots of jumping have 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges grants you a competence bonus on Jump checks for 1 round. 1 charge: Gain a +10 competence bonus on Jump checks. 2 charges: Gain a +15 competence bonus on Jump checks. 3 charges: Gain a +20 competence bonus on Jump checks.

Boots of landing.description: While worn, you land on your feet no matter how far you fall, and you take 2 fewer dice of damage from the fall than normal (thus, a fall of 20 feet or less deals no damage).

Boots of sidestepping.description:
When activated, you can take a 5-foot step even if you have already moved during the round, and it does not prevent you from moving again afterward (or taking your normal 5-foot step).

Functions three times per day.

Boots of skating.description: You can skate along the ground as if on smooth ice, gaining a +10-foot enhancement bonus to your land speed while wearing the boots. Skating up an incline removes the speed bonus; skating down a decline increases the bonus by an additional 10 feet.

Boots of stomping.description:
When you stomp your feet and activate the boots, you generate a 15-foot-long cone-shaped burst of psychokinetic force along the ground. Creatures standing on the ground in the area that fail a DC 13 Reflex save are knocked prone and take 1d4 points of nonlethal damage.

Functions three times per day.

Boots of swift passage.description:
When activated, teleports you up to 20 feet in any direction (with no chance of error). You must have line of sight and line of effect to your destination.

Functions five times per day.

Boots of temporal acceleration.description:
Once per day, you can enter another time frame for 2 rounds, speeding up so greatly that all other creatures seem frozen. Other creatures are normally immune to your attacks, powers, or spells, but area effects that last longer than 2 rounds apply normally once the item's effect expires.

While under the effect, you cannot enter an area protected by an antimagic field, null psionics field, or by a spell or power that neutralizes 9th-level spells or powers. Normal and magical fire, cold, acid, and the like can still harm you. Except as described here, this effect functions as a time stop spell.

When the duration expires, you resume acting during your current turn in normal time, but you are shaken for 1 round.

Boots of the battle charger.description:
When activated, allows you to make a charge attack as a standard action (rather than a full-round action), though the charge only includes movement up to your speed. You must make the charge attack in the round you activate the boots, or the effect is lost.

If you also wear a magic item that grants an enhancement bonus to Dexterity, you can move across difficult terrain and through squares occupied by allies when making the charge.

Functions two times per day.

Boots of the mountain king.description: While worn, you ignore increased movement costs and skill check DC increases for light and dense rubble. You can move up stairs and slopes at normal speed and run or charge downhill without making a Balance check.

Boots of the mountain king, greater.description: These boots function as boots of the mountain king. In addition, once per day you can activate them to gain the benefit of a stoneskin spell.

Boots of the unending journeyR.description:
While worn, you gain a +10-foot enhancement bonus to your speed if you are neutral good, lawful neutral, neutral, chaotic neutral, or neutral evil. This is a continuous effect.

Relic power (worship Fharlanghn and sacrifice a 4th-level divine spell slot, or have True Believer and 7 HD): you are constantly under a pass without trace effect and are immune to fatigue and exhaustion.

Boots of tracklessness.description:
While worn, you leave no tracks (as if affected by pass without trace). This is a continuous effect and requires no activation.

Once per day, you can activate the boots to become invisible (as greater invisibility) for 7 rounds.

Boots of tremorsense.description: The soles of these black boots are unusually wide and their shape strangely cylindrical, like the feet of an elephant. When you activate boots of tremorsense, you gain tremorsense out to 30 feet for 5 rounds. This ability functions three times per day.

Acrobat boots.description:
A pair of acrobat boots grants you a +2 competence bonus on Tumble checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation.

 In addition, these boots have 3 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. 3/day.

 Spending 1 or more charges grants you an enhancement bonus to your speed for 1 round.
 1 charge: +10-foot enhancement bonus. 
2 charges: +15-foot enhancement bonus. 
3 charges: +20-foot enhancement bonus.

Dimension stride boots.description: These sleek leather boots lean slightly forward, as if poised to leap. While wearing dimension stride boots, you gain a +2 competence bonus on Jump checks. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. In addition, the boots have 5 charges, which are renewed each day at dawn. Spending 1 or more charges allows you to teleport a short distance (with no chance of error). You must have line of sight and line of effect to your destination. You can’t use the boots to move into a space occupied by another creature, nor can you teleport into a solid object; if you attempt to do so, the boots’ activation is wasted. You can bring along objects weighing up to your maximum load, but you can’t bring other creatures. 1 charge: Teleport 20 feet. 3 charges: Teleport 40 feet. 5 charges: Teleport 60 feet.

Gwaeron’s boots.description: While wearing these boots, affected as if by pass without trace. In addition, you produce no smell and cannot be detected by the scent extraordinary ability.

Quicksilver boots.description:
When activated, move a distance up to your land speed (as if using a move action). Follows normal movement rules except you can move across water or other liquid without falling in (if you start and finish on solid ground). Provokes attacks of opportunity as normal, but rapid travel makes your form blurry, providing concealment against such attacks.

Functions two times per day.

Riding boots.description: While mounted, gain a +5 competence bonus on Ride checks made during combat, plus the benefit of Ride-By Attack even if you lack prerequisites. If you have Spirited Charge, mounted lance charge attacks deal ×4 damage on a critical hit (instead of ×3).

Rock boots.description: Made from heavy leather, these boots have solid iron plating along the toes and ankles. A smoky crystal shard is set into the back of each boot, a few inches above the heel. Rock boots feel rigid, though they do not signifi cantly impede movement. They grant you a +4 bonus on Strength checks made to resist being bull rushed or tripped when standing on the ground. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. When activated, rock boots allow you to ignore any additional movement costs for moving over diffi cult terrain for 1 round. This effect functions fi ve times per day.

Skirmisher boots.description: These comfortable boots are made from soft doeskin. Scouts (CAd 10) prize skirmisher boots, but any character can use them to increase combat prowess in a mobile battle. You gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls when you make a skirmish attack. This is a continuous effect and requires no activation. A character without the skirmish class feature does not gain this benefi t. In addition, you can activate skirmisher boots to make a single extra melee or ranged attack using your full base attack bonus. You can only activate this ability if you have already moved at least 10 feet from the space where you started your turn. Movement while mounted does not allow you to activate the boots. This benefi t does not require the skirmish class feature and can be activated two times per day.

Steadfast boots.description: These thick, heavy boots are reinforced with iron along the sole. Steadfast boots help keep you on your feet.
